message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Execution Plan: 10 Issues as Parallel PRs

## Context

We have 10 GitHub issues (#6-#15) for awesome-list-radar. Each needs: branch, implementation, tests, PR, greptile code review, feedback fixes, squash-merge. All 10 can be implemented in parallel via git worktrees since each branches from `main`. Merge conflicts (primarily in `config.ts`) are resolved at merge time in wave order.

## Issue-to-GitHub# Mapping

| Plan# | GH# | Title | Size | Branch |
|-------|------|-------|------|--------|
| 1 | #6 | Extend CandidateMetadata | S | `feat/6-extend-metadata` |
| 2 | #7 | GitHub pagination | M | `feat/7-github-pagination` |
| 3 | #8 | ArXiv + Web Pages config | S | `feat/8-source-configurability` |
| 4 | #9 | Filter system | M | `feat/9-filter-system` |
| 5 | #10 | Smart classification | M | `feat/10-smart-classification` |
| 6 | #11 | Custom issue template | M | `feat/11-issue-template` |
| 7 | #12 | Registry sources | L | `feat/12-registry-sources` |
| 8 | #13 | Retry utility | S | `feat/13-retry-utility` |
| 9 | #14 | Incremental state | M | `feat/14-incremental-state` |
| 10 | #15 | Cost control | S | `feat/15-cost-control` |

## Merge Order (Waves)

Config.ts is touched by all 10 issues. `llm.ts` by 4 issues. Wave ordering prevents semantic conflicts.

| Wave | PRs | Rationale |
|------|-----|-----------|
| 1 | #6, #13 | #6 adds types others consume. #13 is additive (new file + wrapping). |
| 2 | #7, #8, #15 | Extend source configs + cost control. Independent schema sections. |
| 3 | #9, #10, #11 | Filter, classification, output. Use metadata fields from #6. |
| 4 | #12, #14 | Registry (new source) + state (orchestration). Most independent. |

After merging each wave, later-wave PRs rebase onto updated `main` before merging.

## Phase 1: Parallel Implementation (10 worktree agents)

Launch all 10 agents simultaneously, each with `isolation: "worktree"` and `run_in_background: true`.

### Shared Worker Instructions

Each worker receives:
1. Project context (structure, conventions, commands)
2. Issue-specific scope boundary and implementation steps
3. Post-implementation checklist:
   - `bun run lint` (type check)
   - `bun test` (all tests pass)
   - `bun run build` (ncc bundle)
   - Commit: `feat: <description> (closes #N)`
   - `git push -u origin <branch>`
   - `gh pr create --title "<title>" --body "<description>"`

### Issue-Specific Scopes

**#6 (Metadata):** Add `license`, `archived`, `fork`, `owner`, `homepage`, `lastCommitAt` to `CandidateMetadata` in types.ts. Populate from GitHub API in github.ts. Add XML tags in llm.ts `buildUserPrompt()`. Add table rows in issues.ts `buildIssueBody()`. Tests: github.test.ts, llm.test.ts, issues.test.ts.

**#7 (GitHub pagination):** Add `max_results` (max 1000), `sort`, `exclude_forks`, `exclude_archived` to `GithubSourceSchema`. Implement pagination loop in github.ts. Append `fork:false`/`archived:false` to query. Tests: github.test.ts, config.test.ts.

**#8 (Source config):** ArXiv: configurable `max_results`, optional `date_range`. Web pages: configurable `extraction_prompt`, `model`, `request_timeout`, `user_agent`. Tests: arxiv.test.ts, web_pages.test.ts, config.test.ts.

**#9 (Filter system):** New `FilterSchema` with `include`/`require_all`/`exclude` keywords + metadata filters (`exclude_forks`, `exclude_archived`, `require_license`, `max_age_days`). Enhanced URL normalization in dedup.ts. New `src/filter/metadata.ts`. Tests: keywords.test.ts, dedup.test.ts, new metadata.test.ts.

**#10 (Classification):** Add `system_prompt`, `context`, `max_description_length`, `categories[]` to ClassificationSchema. New `src/utils/parse_list.ts` to extract ##/### headers from list_file. Include category tree in LLM prompt. Tests: llm.test.ts, new parse_list.test.ts.

**#11 (Issue template):** Add `title_prefix`, `include_fields`, `suggested_entry_format` to IssueTemplateSchema. Implement `{{var}}` interpolation. Pass config to `buildIssueTitle`/`buildIssueBody`. Tests: issues.test.ts.

**#12 (Registry):** New `src/sources/registry.ts` with npm/PyPI/crates.io collectors. Add `"registry"` to source union in types.ts. New `RegistrySourceSchema` in config.ts. Wire up in index.ts. Tests: new registry.test.ts.

**#13 (Retry):** New `src/utils/retry.ts` with `withRetry()` (exponential backoff + jitter + rate limit detection). Wrap API calls in github.ts, arxiv.ts, blogs.ts, web_pages.ts, llm.ts, issues.ts. Tests: new retry.test.ts.

**#14 (State):** New `src/state.ts` with `loadState`/`saveState`/`filterSeenCandidates`/`recordCandidates`. State file: `.radar-state.json`. Add `state_file` to config. Integrate in index.ts. Tests: new state.test.ts.

**#15 (Cost):** Track `message.usage.input_tokens`/`output_tokens` from Anthropic API. Log per-candidate + cumulative cost. Add `max_budget_usd`, `max_classifications_per_run` to ClassificationSchema. Keep `max_issues_per_run` as deprecated alias. Tests: llm.test.ts.

## Phase 2: Code Review (greptile)

After each PR is created:
1. Trigger greptile code review: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__trigger_code_review`
2. Poll for completion: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__get_code_review`
3. Read comments: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__list_merge_request_comments`
4. Resume the worker agent to address feedback (push fix commits)
5. Re-verify: `bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`

## Phase 3: Merge (wave order)

For each wave:
1. Squash-merge PRs in wave using `mcp__plugin_github_github__merge_pull_request` with `merge_method: "squash"`
2. After wave merges, later-wave PR branches need conflict resolution (rebase or merge main into branch)
3. Re-run CI on updated branches before merging next wave

## File Conflict Map

| File | Issues | Resolution |
|------|--------|------------|
| `config.ts` | All 10 | Each adds distinct schema section. Textual conflicts at merge. |
| `types.ts` | #6, #12 | #6 adds metadata fields, #12 adds source type. Orthogonal. |
| `github.ts` | #6, #7, #13 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 2: #7. Sequential. |
| `llm.ts` | #6, #10, #13, #15 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 2: #15, Wave 3: #10. Sequential. |
| `issues.ts` | #6, #11, #13 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 3: #11. Sequential. |
| `index.ts` | #12, #14 | Wave 4: both. Orthogonal additions. |

## Verification

Per-PR: `bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`
Per-wave (after merge to main): `bun install && bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`
Final: All 84+ original tests pass + new tests for all 10 features.


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/moventsai/.claude/projects/-Users-moventsai-Projects-mine-awesome-list-radar/199cdb89-c906-4be2-b6ee-cb0e5cfc91c3.jsonl

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/moventsai/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents

# Dispatching Parallel Agents

## Overview

When you have multiple unrelated failures (different test files, different subsystems, different bugs), investigating them sequentially wastes time. Each investigation is independent and can happen in parallel.

**Core principle:** Dispatch one agent per independent problem domain. Let them work concurrently.

## When to Use

```dot
digraph when_to_use {
    "Multiple failures?" [shape=diamond];
    "Are they independent?" [shape=diamond];
    "Single agent investigates all" [shape=box];
    "One agent per problem domain" [shape=box];
    "Can they work in parallel?" [shape=diamond];
    "Sequential agents" [shape=box];
    "Parallel dispatch" [shape=box];

    "Multiple failures?" -> "Are they independent?" [label="yes"];
    "Are they independent?" -> "Single agent investigates all" [label="no - related"];
    "Are they independent?" -> "Can they work in parallel?" [label="yes"];
    "Can they work in parallel?" -> "Parallel dispatch" [label="yes"];
    "Can they work in parallel?" -> "Sequential agents" [label="no - shared state"];
}
```

**Use when:**
- 3+ test files failing with different root causes
- Multiple subsystems broken independently
- Each problem can be understood without context from others
- No shared state between investigations

**Don't use when:**
- Failures are related (fix one might fix others)
- Need to understand full system state
- Agents would interfere with each other

## The Pattern

### 1. Identify Independent Domains

Group failures by what's broken:
- File A tests: Tool approval flow
- File B tests: Batch completion behavior
- File C tests: Abort functionality

Each domain is independent - fixing tool approval doesn't affect abort tests.

### 2. Create Focused Agent Tasks

Each agent gets:
- **Specific scope:** One test file or subsystem
- **Clear goal:** Make these tests pass
- **Constraints:** Don't change other code
- **Expected output:** Summary of what you found and fixed

### 3. Dispatch in Parallel

```typescript
// In Claude Code / AI environment
Task("Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts failures")
Task("Fix batch-completion-behavior.test.ts failures")
Task("Fix tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts failures")
// All three run concurrently
```

### 4. Review and Integrate

When agents return:
- Read each summary
- Verify fixes don't conflict
- Run full test suite
- Integrate all changes

## Agent Prompt Structure

Good agent prompts are:
1. **Focused** - One clear problem domain
2. **Self-contained** - All context needed to understand the problem
3. **Specific about output** - What should the agent return?

```markdown
Fix the 3 failing tests in src/agents/agent-tool-abort.test.ts:

1. "should abort tool with partial output capture" - expects 'interrupted at' in message
2. "should handle mixed completed and aborted tools" - fast tool aborted instead of completed
3. "should properly track pendingToolCount" - expects 3 results but gets 0

These are timing/race condition issues. Your task:

1. Read the test file and understand what each test verifies
2. Identify root cause - timing issues or actual bugs?
3. Fix by:
   - Replacing arbitrary timeouts with event-based waiting
   - Fixing bugs in abort implementation if found
   - Adjusting test expectations if testing changed behavior

Do NOT just increase timeouts - find the real issue.

Return: Summary of what you found and what you fixed.
```

## Common Mistakes

**❌ Too broad:** "Fix all the tests" - agent gets lost
**✅ Specific:** "Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts" - focused scope

**❌ No context:** "Fix the race condition" - agent doesn't know where
**✅ Context:** Paste the error messages and test names

**❌ No constraints:** Agent might refactor everything
**✅ Constraints:** "Do NOT change production code" or "Fix tests only"

**❌ Vague output:** "Fix it" - you don't know what changed
**✅ Specific:** "Return summary of root cause and changes"

## When NOT to Use

**Related failures:** Fixing one might fix others - investigate together first
**Need full context:** Understanding requires seeing entire system
**Exploratory debugging:** You don't know what's broken yet
**Shared state:** Agents would interfere (editing same files, using same resources)

## Real Example from Session

**Scenario:** 6 test failures across 3 files after major refactoring

**Failures:**
- agent-tool-abort.test.ts: 3 failures (timing issues)
- batch-completion-behavior.test.ts: 2 failures (tools not executing)
- tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts: 1 failure (execution count = 0)

**Decision:** Independent domains - abort logic separate from batch completion separate from race conditions

**Dispatch:**
```
Agent 1 → Fix agent-tool-abort.test.ts
Agent 2 → Fix batch-completion-behavior.test.ts
Agent 3 → Fix tool-approval-race-conditions.test.ts
```

**Results:**
- Agent 1: Replaced timeouts with event-based waiting
- Agent 2: Fixed event structure bug (threadId in wrong place)
- Agent 3: Added wait for async tool execution to complete

**Integration:** All fixes independent, no conflicts, full suite green

**Time saved:** 3 problems solved in parallel vs sequentially

## Key Benefits

1. **Parallelization** - Multiple investigations happen simultaneously
2. **Focus** - Each agent has narrow scope, less context to track
3. **Independence** - Agents don't interfere with each other
4. **Speed** - 3 problems solved in time of 1

## Verification

After agents return:
1. **Review each summary** - Understand what changed
2. **Check for conflicts** - Did agents edit same code?
3. **Run full suite** - Verify all fixes work together
4. **Spot check** - Agents can make systematic errors

## Real-World Impact

From debugging session (2025-10-03):
- 6 failures across 3 files
- 3 agents dispatched in parallel
- All investigations completed concurrently
- All fixes integrated successfully
- Zero conflicts between agent changes
Now let me first understand the project structure and read key files before dispatching agents.

input.command: cat package.json | head -30 && echo "---" && cat tsconfig.json 2>/dev/null | head -30

content.content:
{
  "name": "awesome-list-radar",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "GitHub Action that discovers relevant content for awesome lists and files issues for maintainer triage",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "build": "rm -rf dist && ncc build src/index.ts -o dist --source-map --license licenses.txt",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:watch": "vitest",
    "lint": "tsc --noEmit"
  },
  "keywords": [
    "github-action",
    "awesome-list",
    "curation",
    "radar"
  ],
  "license": "MIT",
  "dependencies": {
    "@actions/core": "^1.11.1",
    "@actions/github": "^6.0.0",
    "@anthropic-ai/sdk": "^0.39.0",
    "@octokit/rest": "^21.1.0",
    "fast-xml-parser": "^4.5.1",

{"number":6,"title":"Extend CandidateMetadata with universal fields","body":"## Summary

Add universal metadata fields to `CandidateMetadata` that are available from the GitHub API but not currently captured. These fields are essential for diverse awesome-list support (filtering by license, excluding archived/fork repos, etc.).

## Fields to Add

Add the following to `CandidateMetadata` in `src/sources/types.ts`:

- `license` — SPDX identifier (e.g. `"MIT"`, `"Apache-2.0"`)
- `archived` — boolean
- `fork` — boolean
- `owner` — string (GitHub username or org)
- `homepage` — string (project homepage URL)
- `lastCommitAt` — ISO 8601 date string of last commit/push

## Implementation

1. **Schema** (`src/sources/types.ts`): Add optional fields to `CandidateMetadata`
2. **GitHub source** (`src/sources/github.ts`): Populate new fields from the existing API response (data is already returned, just not captured)
3. **LLM prompt** (`src/classifier/llm.ts`): Add new fields as XML tags in the candidate description sent to the classifier
4. **Issue body** (`src/output/issues.ts`): Include new fields in the metadata table of created issues

## Scope Boundary

- **Owns:** `src/sources/types.ts` (schema), downstream consumers updated to use new fields
- **Does NOT own:** How sources fetch data (Issue #2) or how filters use metadata (Issue #4)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All new fields defined as optional in `CandidateMetadata`
- [ ] GitHub source populates all fields from API response
- [ ] LLM prompt includes new fields when available
- [ ] Issue body displays new metadata fields
- [ ] Existing tests pass, new tests for field population
- [ ] Type check and build pass","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/moven0831/awesome-list-radar/issues/6","user":{"login":"moven0831","id":60170228,"profile_url":"https://github.com/moven0831","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/60170228?v=4"},"author_association":"OWNER","labels":["P0","data-model","size:S"],"reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-03-05T09:31:33Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T09:31:33Z"}

{"number":7,"title":"GitHub source — pagination, filtering, and configurability","body":"## Summary

The GitHub source currently fetches a single page of 100 results with no configurability. For diverse awesome-lists with broad search terms, this misses many candidates. Add pagination support and configurable filtering.

## Changes

### Config additions (`GithubSourceSchema` in `src/config.ts`)
- `max_results` — integer, default 100, max 1000 (GitHub search API limit)
- `sort` — enum: `"stars"`, `"updated"`, `"best-match"` (default)
- `exclude_forks` — boolean, default false
- `exclude_archived` — boolean, default false

### Pagination (`src/sources/github.ts`)
- Implement pagination loop: fetch pages of 100 until `max_results` reached or no more results
- Respect GitHub's `Link` header or use `page` parameter

### Query filtering
- When `exclude_forks: true`, append `fork:false` to the search query
- When `exclude_archived: true`, append `archived:false` to the search query

## Scope Boundary

- **Owns:** `src/sources/github.ts`, `GithubSourceSchema` in `src/config.ts`
- **Does NOT own:** What metadata fields exist (Issue #1) or how metadata is filtered (Issue #4)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pagination fetches up to `max_results` across multiple pages
- [ ] `sort`, `exclude_forks`, `exclude_archived` configs work correctly
- [ ] Default behavior unchanged (100 results, no fork/archive filtering)
- [ ] Tests cover pagination and query building
- [ ] Type check and build pass","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/moven0831/awesome-list-radar/issues/7","user":{"login":"moven0831","id":60170228,"profile_url":"https://github.com/moven0831","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/60170228?v=4"},"author_association":"OWNER","labels":["P0","collect-github","size:M"],"reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-03-05T09:31:34Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T09:31:34Z"}

{"number":8,"title":"Non-GitHub source configurability (ArXiv + Web Pages)","body":"## Summary

ArXiv and Web Pages sources have hardcoded values that limit their usefulness for diverse lists. Make them configurable.

## Changes

### ArXiv (`src/sources/arxiv.ts`)
- Make `max_results` configurable (currently hardcoded to 50)
- Add optional `date_range` config (`{ start: string, end: string }`) for filtering by publication date

### Web Pages (`src/sources/web_pages.ts`)
- Make LLM extraction prompt configurable (currently hardcoded to "articles or blog posts")
- Make model configurable (currently hardcoded to Haiku)
- Add configurable `request_timeout` (default 30s) and `user_agent` header

### Config schemas
- Update `ArxivSourceSchema` and `WebPagesSourceSchema` in `src/config.ts`

## Scope Boundary

- **Owns:** `src/sources/arxiv.ts`, `src/sources/web_pages.ts`, their config schemas
- **Does NOT own:** RSS/blog source (no changes needed), GitHub source (Issue #2)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ArXiv `max_results` and `date_range` configurable
- [ ] Web pages extraction prompt, model, timeout, and user-agent configurable
- [ ] Defaults match current behavior (backward compatible)
- [ ] Tests cover new config options
- [ ] Type check and build pass","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/moven0831/awesome-list-radar/issues/8","user":{"login":"moven0831","id":60170228,"profile_url":"https://github.com/moven0831","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/60170228?v=4"},"author_association":"OWNER","labels":["P0","size:S","collect-other"],"reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-03-05T09:31:35Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T09:31:35Z"}

content.text:
{"number":9,"title":"Configurable filter system (AND/OR/NOT keywords + metadata filters)","body":"## Summary

The current filter only supports OR-based substring matching and dedup is limited to 100 open issues. For diverse awesome-lists, we need expressive keyword filters, metadata-based filtering, and robust dedup.

## Changes

### Keyword filters (`src/filter/keywords.ts`)
Replace OR-only substring matching with three modes:
- `include` — OR match (current behavior, default)
- `require_all` — AND match (all keywords must match)
- `exclude` — NOT match (reject if any keyword matches)

### Metadata filters (new `src/filter/metadata.ts`)
- `exclude_forks: true` — filter out fork repos
- `exclude_archived: true` — filter out archived repos
- `require_license: true` — require a license field
- `max_age_days: N` — filter by last activity date

### Robust dedup (`src/filter/dedup.ts`)
- URL normalization: strip protocol, remove `www.`, trailing slash, tracking params (`utm_*`, `ref`, etc.)
- Paginate issue listing (currently limited to 100 open issues)
- Also check closed issues to avoid re-suggesting rejected candidates

### Config (`FilterSchema` in `src/config.ts`)
Update schema to support new filter options.

## Scope Boundary

- **Owns:** `src/filter/keywords.ts`, `src/filter/dedup.ts`, new `src/filter/metadata.ts`, `FilterSchema` in config
- **Does NOT own:** What metadata fields exist (Issue #1) or how sources populate them (Issues #2, #3)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AND/OR/NOT keyword filtering works correctly
- [ ] Metadata filters applied when configured
- [ ] URL normalization handles edge cases (protocol, www, trailing slash, tracking params)
- [ ] Dedup paginates through all issues (open + closed)
- [ ] Backward compatible defaults
- [ ] Tests for all filter modes and dedup normalization
- [ ] Type check and build pass","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/moven0831/awesome-list-radar/issues/9","user":{"login":"moven0831","id":60170228,"profile_url":"https://github.com/moven0831","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/60170228?v=4"},"author_association":"OWNER","labels":["P0","size:M","filter"],"reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-03-05T09:31:38Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T09:31:38Z"}

content.text:
{"number":10,"title":"Smart classification — configurable prompts + auto-extracted categories","body":"## Summary

The classifier currently uses a fixed prompt with no awareness of the target list's category structure. For diverse awesome-lists with 30-100+ nested sections, the LLM needs category context to suggest accurate placement. This is the biggest accuracy improvement.

## Changes

### Classification config (`ClassificationSchema` in `src/config.ts`)
- `system_prompt` — optional override for the system prompt
- `context` — optional string appended to the prompt (e.g. "This list focuses on self-hosted alternatives")
- `max_description_length` — truncate long descriptions before sending to LLM
- `categories[]` — explicit list of valid categories/sections

### Auto-extract categories (new `src/utils/parse_list.ts`)
- Parse `##` and `###` markdown headers from the configured `list_file`
- Build a category tree (nested structure)
- When no explicit `categories[]` configured, auto-extract from the list file

### LLM prompt enhancement (`src/classifier/llm.ts`)
- Include the category tree in the classification prompt
- Instruct the LLM to pick from the available categories
- Apply `max_description_length` truncation
- Apply optional `context` and `system_prompt` overrides

## Scope Boundary

- **Owns:** `src/classifier/llm.ts`, new `src/utils/parse_list.ts`, `ClassificationSchema` in config
- **Does NOT own:** What metadata the LLM receives (Issue #1 adds fields, this issue formats the prompt)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Category auto-extraction from markdown files works for various header formats
- [ ] Explicit `categories[]` config overrides auto-extraction
- [ ] LLM prompt includes category tree
- [ ] `system_prompt`, `context`, `max_description_length` configs work
- [ ] Tests for markdown parsing and prompt generation
- [ ] Type check and build pass","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/moven0831/awesome-list-radar/issues/10","user":{"login":"moven0831","id":60170228,"profile_url":"https://github.com/moven0831","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/60170228?v=4"},"author_association":"OWNER","labels":["P0","size:M","classify"],"reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-03-05T09:31:48Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T09:31:48Z"}

content.text:
{"number":11,"title":"Customizable issue template","body":"## Summary

Different awesome-lists have different conventions for how entries are formatted and what metadata matters. Allow list maintainers to customize the issue output.

## Changes

### Issue template config (`IssueTemplateSchema` in `src/config.ts`)
- `title_prefix` — string, default `"[Radar]"`
- `include_fields` — array of metadata field names to display in the issue body (e.g. `["stars", "license", "language", "lastCommitAt"]`)
- `suggested_entry_format` — template string for the suggested list entry (e.g. `"- [{{name}}]({{url}}) - {{description}}. \`{{license}}\`"`)

### Issue output (`src/output/issues.ts`)
- Use `title_prefix` instead of hardcoded prefix
- Only render metadata rows for fields listed in `include_fields` (default: all available)
- Render `suggested_entry_format` with candidate data using simple template interpolation

- **Owns:** `src/output/issues.ts`, `IssueTemplateSchema` in config
- **Does NOT own:** What metadata exists (Issue #1) or how candidates are classified (Issue #5)

- [ ] `include_fields` controls which metadata rows appear

- [ ] Defaults match current behavior (backward compatible)

content.text:
{"number":12,"title":"Package registry sources (npm, PyPI, crates.io)","body":"## Summary

Many awesome-lists track packages from language-specific registries. Add collectors for npm, PyPI, and crates.io to discover candidates from package registries.

## Changes

### New source (`src/sources/registry.ts`)
Implement collectors for three registries:
- **npm**: Use `https://registry.npmjs.org/-/v1/search` API
- **PyPI**: Use `https://pypi.org/search/` or XML-RPC API
- **crates.io**: Use `https://crates.io/api/v1/crates` API

Each collector:
- Searches by configured keywords
- Maps results to `Candidate` with `source: "registry"`
- Populates metadata: `registryUrl`, `downloads`, `version`, `license`, `description`

### Config (`RegistrySourceSchema` in `src/config.ts`)
```yaml
sources:
  registries:
    - type: npm
      keywords: ["selfhosted", "docker"]
      min_downloads: 1000
    - type: pypi
      keywords: ["machine-learning"]
      min_downloads: 500
```

### Source type
- Add `"registry"` to the source type union

## Scope Boundary

- **Owns:** New `src/sources/registry.ts`, `RegistrySourceSchema` in config, `"registry"` source type
- **Does NOT own:** Existing sources or how candidates are filtered/classified

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] npm, PyPI, and crates.io collectors implemented
- [ ] Results mapped to `Candidate` with appropriate metadata
- [ ] `min_downloads` filter works per registry
- [ ] Config schema validates registry source options
- [ ] Tests for each registry collector (with mocked API responses)
- [ ] Type check and build pass","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/moven0831/awesome-list-radar/issues/12","user":{"login":"moven0831","id":60170228,"profile_url":"https://github.com/moven0831","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/60170228?v=4"},"author_association":"OWNER","labels":["P1","collect-registry","size:L"],"reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-03-05T09:32:10Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T09:32:10Z"}

content.text:
{"number":13,"title":"Retry with exponential backoff + rate limit awareness","body":"## Summary

External API calls (GitHub, Anthropic, RSS feeds, web pages) can fail transiently or hit rate limits. Add a generic retry utility to improve reliability across all pipeline stages.

## Changes

### Retry utility (new `src/utils/retry.ts`)
- `withRetry(fn: () => Promise, options?: RetryOptions): Promise`
- Exponential backoff with jitter (default: 3 retries, 1s base delay)
- Detect HTTP 429 status and `X-RateLimit-Remaining: 0` header
- On rate limit: wait for `X-RateLimit-Reset` or `Retry-After` header duration
- Configurable: `maxRetries`, `baseDelay`, `maxDelay`, `retryableStatuses`

### Integration points
Apply `withRetry()` to all external calls:
- GitHub API searches (`src/sources/github.ts`)
- Anthropic API classification calls (`src/classifier/llm.ts`)
- RSS feed fetches (`src/sources/rss.ts`)
- Web page fetches (`src/sources/web_pages.ts`)
- ArXiv API calls (`src/sources/arxiv.ts`)
- GitHub issue creation (`src/output/issues.ts`)

## Scope Boundary

- **Owns:** New `src/utils/retry.ts`, integration points in all external call sites
- **Does NOT own:** The logic of any stage — just wraps external calls

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `withRetry()` utility with exponential backoff and jitter
- [ ] Rate limit detection (429, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `Retry-After`)
- [ ] Applied to all external API calls
- [ ] Tests for retry logic, backoff timing, rate limit handling
- [ ] Type check and build pass","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/moven0831/awesome-list-radar/issues/13","user":{"login":"moven0831","id":60170228,"profile_url":"https://github.com/moven0831","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/60170228?v=4"},"author_association":"OWNER","labels":["size:S","P1","reliability"],"reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-03-05T09:32:18Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T09:32:18Z"}

content.text:
{"number":14,"title":"Incremental state / watermark system","body":"## Summary

Each run currently re-processes all candidates from scratch, wasting LLM tokens on previously seen items. Add a persistent state file to track seen URLs and per-source watermarks.

## Changes

### State file (new `src/state.ts`)
- Read/write `.radar-state.json` (configurable via `state_file` in config)
- Schema:
  ```json
  {
    "seen_urls": {
      "https://github.com/foo/bar": { "status": "accepted", "seen_at": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z" },
      "https://github.com/baz/qux": { "status": "rejected", "seen_at": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z" }
    },
    "watermarks": {
      "github:0": { "last_run": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z", "last_result_id": "..." },
      "arxiv:0": { "last_run": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z" }
    }
  }
  ```

### Integration (`src/index.ts`)
- Load state at pipeline start
- After collection, filter out candidates whose URLs are already in `seen_urls` (before classification)
- After classification + issue creation, update `seen_urls` with new candidates and their status
- Update per-source watermarks after each source completes
- Save state at pipeline end

### Config
- `state_file` — string, default `.radar-state.json`

## Scope Boundary

- **Owns:** New `src/state.ts`, state loading/saving in `src/index.ts`, state-based filtering integration
- **Does NOT own:** URL normalization or dedup logic (Issue #4) — state provides an additional URL set to check against

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] State file read/written correctly
- [ ] Previously seen URLs skipped before classification
- [ ] Accepted and rejected candidates both recorded
- [ ] Per-source watermarks tracked
- [ ] `state_file` config option works
- [ ] Graceful handling of missing/corrupt state file
- [ ] Tests for state operations
- [ ] Type check and build pass","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/moven0831/awesome-list-radar/issues/14","user":{"login":"moven0831","id":60170228,"profile_url":"https://github.com/moven0831","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/60170228?v=4"},"author_association":"OWNER","labels":["size:M","P1","state"],"reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-03-05T09:32:44Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T09:32:44Z"}

content.text:
{"number":15,"title":"LLM cost estimation and budget control","body":"## Summary

Users have no visibility into LLM costs and no way to cap spending. Track token usage and add budget controls.

## Changes

### Token tracking (`src/classifier/llm.ts`)
- Extract `input_tokens` and `output_tokens` from Anthropic API `message.usage` response
- Log per-candidate token usage and estimated cost (using model-specific pricing)
- Track cumulative totals across all classifications in a run
- Log final summary: total input tokens, output tokens, estimated cost

### Budget control (`ClassificationSchema` in `src/config.ts`)
- `max_budget_usd` — optional float, abort classification early if estimated cost exceeds budget
- Before each classification call, check cumulative cost against budget
- If budget exceeded, log warning and skip remaining candidates

### Rename config field
- Rename `max_issues_per_run` to `max_classifications_per_run` (more accurate name)
- Keep `max_issues_per_run` as a deprecated alias for backward compatibility

## Scope Boundary

- **Owns:** Cost tracking in `src/classifier/llm.ts`, budget config in `ClassificationSchema`
- **Does NOT own:** Classification logic or prompt (Issue #5)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Per-candidate and cumulative token usage logged
- [ ] Cost estimation uses correct per-model pricing
- [ ] `max_budget_usd` aborts early when exceeded
- [ ] `max_classifications_per_run` rename with deprecated alias
- [ ] Tests for cost tracking and budget enforcement
- [ ] Type check and build pass","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/moven0831/awesome-list-radar/issues/15","user":{"login":"moven0831","id":60170228,"profile_url":"https://github.com/moven0831","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/60170228?v=4"},"author_association":"OWNER","labels":["size:S","P1","cost"],"reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-03-05T09:32:46Z","updated_at":"2026-03-05T09:32:46Z"}

  GPU-accelerated zero-knowledge cryptography for consumer devices

list_file: README.md

sources:
  github:
    topics: [webgpu, gpu-crypto, msm, ntt, zero-knowledge]
    languages: [rust, cuda, metal, wgsl]
    min_stars: 5
    created_after: "30d"
  arxiv:
    categories: [cs.CR, cs.DC]
    keywords: [GPU, MSM, NTT, zero-knowledge, proving]
  blogs:
    feeds:
      - https://blog.example.com/feed.xml
    keywords: [gpu proving, client-side zk, webgpu crypto]

classification:
  model: claude-sonnet-4-6
  threshold: 70
  max_issues_per_run: 5

issue_template:
  labels: [radar, needs-review]

input.prompt:
# Issue #6: Extend CandidateMetadata with universal fields

## Branch: `feat/6-extend-metadata`

Create branch `feat/6-extend-metadata` from main, implement, test, commit, push, and create PR.

## Project Context

- TypeScript project using Bun, Vitest for tests, Zod for schemas
- Source files in `src/`, tests in `tests/` (mirroring src structure)
- Tests use `vi.mock("@actions/core", ...)` pattern
- Config in `src/config.ts`, types in `src/sources/types.ts`
- Build: `bun run build` (ncc), Lint: `bun run lint` (tsc --noEmit), Test: `bun test`

## What to Implement

Add these optional fields to `CandidateMetadata` in `src/sources/types.ts`:
- `license?: string` — SPDX identifier (e.g. "MIT", "Apache-2.0")
- `archived?: boolean`
- `fork?: boolean`
- `owner?: string` — GitHub username or org
- `homepage?: string` — project homepage URL
- `lastCommitAt?: string` — ISO 8601 date string

### Files to modify:

1. **`src/sources/types.ts`**: Add the 6 new optional fields to `CandidateMetadata`

2. **`src/sources/github.ts`**: Populate new fields from API response in `collectGitHub()`. The GitHub search API already returns these fields on each repo item:
   - `repo.license?.spdx_id` → `license`
   - `repo.archived` → `archived`
   - `repo.fork` → `fork`
   - `repo.owner.login` → `owner`
   - `repo.homepage` → `homepage`
   - `repo.pushed_at` → `lastCommitAt`

3. **`src/classifier/llm.ts`**: In `buildUserPrompt()`, add XML tags for new fields when available:
   - `<candidate_license>...</candidate_license>`
   - `<candidate_archived>...</candidate_archived>`
   - `<candidate_fork>...</candidate_fork>`
   - `<candidate_owner>...</candidate_owner>`
   - `<candidate_homepage>...</candidate_homepage>`
   - `<candidate_last_commit>...</candidate_last_commit>`

4. **`src/output/issues.ts`**: In `buildIssueBody()`, add table rows for new fields when available:
   - `| **License** | MIT |`
   - `| **Archived** | Yes/No |`
   - `| **Fork** | Yes/No |`
   - `| **Owner** | username |`
   - `| **Homepage** | url |`
   - `| **Last Commit** | date |`

# Issue #7: GitHub source — pagination, filtering, and configurability

## Branch: `feat/7-github-pagination`

Create branch `feat/7-github-pagination` from main, implement, test, commit, push, and create PR.

## Project Context

- TypeScript project using Bun, Vitest for tests, Zod for schemas
- Source files in `src/`, tests in `tests/` (mirroring src structure)
- Tests use `vi.mock("@actions/core", ...)` pattern
- Config in `src/config.ts` using Zod schemas
- Build: `bun run build` (ncc), Lint: `bun run lint` (tsc --noEmit), Test: `bun test`

## Current State

`src/config.ts` has `GithubSourceSchema`:
```ts
const GithubSourceSchema = z.object({
  topics: z.array(z.string()).min(1),
  languages: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
  min_stars: z.number().int().nonnegative().default(0),
  created_after: z.string().regex(/^\d+d$/, 'Must be in format "Nd" (e.g. "30d")').default("30d"),
});
```

`src/sources/github.ts` fetches a single page of 100 results sorted by stars.

## What to Implement

### 1. Config additions (`src/config.ts` - `GithubSourceSchema`)
Add these fields:
- `max_results` — `z.number().int().min(1).max(1000).default(100)`
- `sort` — `z.enum(["stars", "updated", "best-match"]).default("stars")`
- `exclude_forks` — `z.boolean().default(false)`
- `exclude_archived` — `z.boolean().default(false)`

### 2. Pagination (`src/sources/github.ts`)
Replace the single API call with a pagination loop:
```ts
const maxResults = gh.max_results; // from config, default 100
const perPage = Math.min(maxResults, 100);
let page = 1;
let fetched = 0;

while (fetched < maxResults) {
  const response = await client.search.repos({
    q: query,
    sort: gh.sort === "best-match" ? undefined : gh.sort,
    order: "desc",
    per_page: perPage,
    page,
  });
  
  for (const repo of response.data.items) {
    if (fetched >= maxResults) break;
    candidates.push({...});
    fetched++;
  }
  
  // No more results
  if (response.data.items.length < perPage) break;
  page++;
}
```

### 3. Query filtering (`src/sources/github.ts` - `buildSearchQuery`)
- When `exclude_forks` is true, append `fork:false` to the query
- When `exclude_archived` is true, append `archived:false` to the query

Access config via `config.sources.github!` which now has `exclude_forks` and `exclude_archived`.

### Tests to update/add (`tests/sources/github.test.ts`, `tests/config.test.ts`):

1. **github.test.ts**:
   - Test pagination: mock octokit to return 100 items on page 1, 50 on page 2, verify all 150 collected when max_results=200
   - Test max_results cap: set max_results=50, verify only 50 candidates returned even though API returns 100
   - Test exclude_forks/exclude_archived appended to query
   - Test sort parameter passed correctly
   - Update `baseConfig` to include new fields with defaults

2. **config.test.ts**:
   - Test new config fields parse correctly
   - Test defaults apply (max_results=100, sort="stars", exclude_forks=false, exclude_archived=false)
   - Test max_results validation (reject >1000, reject 0)

## Post-implementation checklist

Run these commands and fix any issues:
1. `bun run lint` (must pass)
2. `bun test` (all tests must pass)
3. `bun run build` (must succeed)
4. Commit with message: `feat: add GitHub pagination, sort, and fork/archive filtering (closes #7)`
5. `git push -u origin feat/7-github-pagination`
6. Create PR with `gh pr create --base main --title "feat: GitHub source pagination and configurability" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Add `max_results` (up to 1000), `sort`, `exclude_forks`, `exclude_archived` to GitHub config
- Implement pagination loop to fetch beyond 100 results
- Append `fork:false`/`archived:false` to search query when configured
- Default behavior unchanged (100 results, sorted by stars)

Closes #7

## Test plan
- [x] Pagination fetches multiple pages up to max_results
- [x] max_results caps total candidates
- [x] sort parameter passed to API
- [x] exclude_forks/exclude_archived modify search query
- [x] Config validation for new fields
- [x] All existing tests pass
- [x] Type check and build pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"`

# Issue #8: Non-GitHub source configurability (ArXiv + Web Pages)

## Branch: `feat/8-source-configurability`

Create branch `feat/8-source-configurability` from main, implement, test, commit, push, and create PR.

## Project Context

- TypeScript project using Bun, Vitest for tests, Zod for schemas
- Source files in `src/`, tests in `tests/` (mirroring src structure)
- Tests use `vi.mock("@actions/core", ...)` pattern
- Build: `bun run build` (ncc), Lint: `bun run lint` (tsc --noEmit), Test: `bun test`

## Current State

`src/config.ts`:
```ts
const ArxivSourceSchema = z.object({
  categories: z.array(z.string()).min(1),
  keywords: z.array(z.string()).min(1),
});

const WebPagesSourceSchema = z.object({
  urls: z.array(z.string().url()).min(1),
  keywords: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
});
```

`src/sources/arxiv.ts`: hardcoded `max_results=50` in `buildArxivUrl()`.

`src/sources/web_pages.ts`: hardcoded model `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`, hardcoded system prompt, no timeout/user-agent.

## What to Implement

### 1. ArXiv config (`src/config.ts` - `ArxivSourceSchema`)
Add:
- `max_results` — `z.number().int().min(1).max(500).default(50)`
- `date_range` — `z.object({ start: z.string(), end: z.string() }).optional()`

### 2. ArXiv source (`src/sources/arxiv.ts`)
- In `buildArxivUrl()`, use `config.sources.arxiv!.max_results` instead of hardcoded 50. Note: `buildArxivUrl` currently takes just a `query` string param. You'll need to also pass the max_results or change the function signature. Simplest: add a second parameter `maxResults: number`.
- If `date_range` is configured, add `submittedDate` filter to the query (ArXiv API supports `submittedDate:[YYYYMMDDHHNN+TO+YYYYMMDDHHNN]` but simpler: just use the range as-is, append ` AND submittedDate:[${start} TO ${end}]` to the query in `buildArxivQuery`)

### 3. Web Pages config (`src/config.ts` - `WebPagesSourceSchema`)
Add:
- `extraction_prompt` — `z.string().optional()` (overrides the default system prompt)
- `model` — `z.string().default("claude-haiku-4-5-20251001")`
- `request_timeout` — `z.number().int().min(1000).max(120000).default(30000)` (milliseconds)
- `user_agent` — `z.string().optional()`

### 4. Web Pages source (`src/sources/web_pages.ts`)
- Use `config.sources.web_pages!.model` instead of hardcoded model
- If `extraction_prompt` is set, use it as the system prompt
- Pass `request_timeout` as AbortSignal timeout on fetch calls: `{ signal: AbortSignal.timeout(timeout) }`
- Pass `user_agent` as User-Agent header on fetch calls: `{ headers: { "User-Agent": userAgent } }`

### Tests to update/add:

1. **`tests/sources/arxiv.test.ts`**: 
   - Test that `max_results` config is used in URL
   - Test that `date_range` is included in query when configured
   - Update any existing tests if function signatures change

2. **`tests/sources/web_pages.test.ts`**:
   - Test that custom model is passed to Anthropic API
   - Test that custom extraction_prompt overrides system prompt
   - Test that request_timeout is applied to fetch
   - Test that user_agent header is sent

3. **`tests/config.test.ts`**:
   - Test ArXiv new fields parse correctly with defaults
   - Test WebPages new fields parse correctly with defaults

## Post-implementation checklist

Run these commands and fix any issues:
1. `bun run lint` (must pass)
2. `bun test` (all tests must pass)  
3. `bun run build` (must succeed)
4. Commit with message: `feat: make ArXiv and Web Pages sources configurable (closes #8)`
5. `git push -u origin feat/8-source-configurability`
6. Create PR with `gh pr create --base main --title "feat: ArXiv and Web Pages source configurability" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- ArXiv: configurable `max_results` and `date_range`
- Web Pages: configurable `extraction_prompt`, `model`, `request_timeout`, `user_agent`
- Backward compatible defaults match current behavior

Closes #8

## Test plan
- [x] ArXiv max_results used in API URL
- [x] ArXiv date_range filters by publication date
- [x] Web pages custom model/prompt/timeout/user-agent work
- [x] Default behavior unchanged
- [x] Config validation for new fields
- [x] All existing tests pass
- [x] Type check and build pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"`

# Issue #11: Customizable issue template

## Branch: `feat/11-issue-template`

Create branch `feat/11-issue-template` from main, implement, test, commit, push, and create PR.

## Project Context

- TypeScript project using Bun, Vitest for tests, Zod for schemas
- Source files in `src/`, tests in `tests/` (mirroring src structure)
- Tests use `vi.mock("@actions/core", ...)` pattern
- Build: `bun run build` (ncc), Lint: `bun run lint` (tsc --noEmit), Test: `bun test`

## Current State

`src/config.ts` - `IssueTemplateSchema`:
```ts
const IssueTemplateSchema = z.object({
  labels: z.array(z.string()).default(["radar", "needs-review"]),
});
```

`src/output/issues.ts`:
- `buildIssueTitle()` uses hardcoded `[Radar]` prefix
- `buildIssueBody()` renders all available metadata fields
- Suggested entry format is hardcoded: `- [${candidate.title}](${candidate.url}) - ${candidate.description.slice(0, 100)}`

## What to Implement

### 1. Config additions (`src/config.ts` - `IssueTemplateSchema`)
Add:
- `title_prefix` — `z.string().default("[Radar]")`
- `include_fields` — `z.array(z.string()).optional()` (if set, only show these metadata fields)
- `suggested_entry_format` — `z.string().optional()` (template with `{{var}}` placeholders)

### 2. Issue title (`src/output/issues.ts` - `buildIssueTitle`)

Change signature to accept config:
```ts
function buildIssueTitle(candidate: ClassifiedCandidate, config: RadarConfig): string {
  const prefix = config.issue_template.title_prefix;
  return `${prefix} ${escapeTableCell(candidate.title).slice(0, 200)}`;
}
```

Update the call site in `createIssues()` to pass config.

### 3. Issue body metadata (`src/output/issues.ts` - `buildIssueBody`)

Change signature to accept config:
```ts
function buildIssueBody(candidate: ClassifiedCandidate, config: RadarConfig): string {
```

Add include_fields filtering. Define a map of all possible metadata rows:
```ts
const allFields: Record<string, () => string | null> = {
  url: () => `| **URL** | ${escapeTableCell(candidate.url)} |`,
  source: () => `| **Source** | ${escapeTableCell(candidate.source)} |`,
  relevanceScore: () => `| **Relevance Score** | ${candidate.relevanceScore}/100 |`,
  suggestedCategory: () => `| **Suggested Category** | ${escapeTableCell(candidate.suggestedCategory)} |`,
  tags: () => candidate.suggestedTags.length > 0 ? `| **Tags** | ${candidate.suggestedTags.map(t => `\`${escapeTableCell(t)}\``).join(", ")} |` : null,
  stars: () => candidate.metadata.stars !== undefined ? `| **Stars** | ${candidate.metadata.stars} |` : null,
  language: () => candidate.metadata.language ? `| **Language** | ${escapeTableCell(candidate.metadata.language)} |` : null,
  authors: () => candidate.metadata.authors?.length ? `| **Authors** | ${escapeTableCell(candidate.metadata.authors.join(", "))} |` : null,
};

const fieldsToShow = config.issue_template.include_fields ?? Object.keys(allFields);
for (const field of fieldsToShow) {
  if (allFields[field]) {
    const row = allFields[field]();
    if (row) lines.push(row);
  }
}
```

### 4. Suggested entry format (`src/output/issues.ts`)

If `suggested_entry_format` is configured, use it with `{{var}}` interpolation:
```ts
function renderTemplate(template: string, candidate: ClassifiedCandidate): string {
  return template
    .replace(/\{\{name\}\}/g, candidate.title)
    .replace(/\{\{url\}\}/g, candidate.url)
    .replace(/\{\{description\}\}/g, candidate.description.slice(0, 100))
    .replace(/\{\{language\}\}/g, candidate.metadata.language ?? "")
    .replace(/\{\{stars\}\}/g, String(candidate.metadata.stars ?? ""))
    .replace(/\{\{license\}\}/g, (candidate.metadata as any).license ?? "")
    .replace(/\{\{source\}\}/g, candidate.source)
    .replace(/\{\{category\}\}/g, candidate.suggestedCategory);
}
```

In buildIssueBody, replace the hardcoded suggested entry with:
```ts
const suggestedEntry = config.issue_template.suggested_entry_format
  ? renderTemplate(config.issue_template.suggested_entry_format, candidate)
  : `- [${candidate.title}](${candidate.url}) - ${candidate.description.slice(0, 100)}`;
```

### 5. Update call sites

In `createIssues()`, pass config to both `buildIssueTitle` and `buildIssueBody`.

### Tests (`tests/output/issues.test.ts`):

Add tests for:
- Custom title_prefix (e.g. "[New]" instead of "[Radar]")
- include_fields limits which metadata rows appear
- suggested_entry_format renders with candidate data
- Default behavior unchanged (backward compatible)
- Template interpolation handles missing fields gracefully

Also update existing tests that call `buildIssueTitle`/`buildIssueBody` to pass config parameter.

### Tests (`tests/config.test.ts`):
- Test new IssueTemplateSchema fields parse correctly with defaults

## Post-implementation checklist

Run these commands and fix any issues:
1. `bun run lint` (must pass)
2. `bun test` (all tests must pass)
3. `bun run build` (must succeed)
4. Commit with message: `feat: customizable issue template with title prefix, field selection, and entry format (closes #11)`
5. `git push -u origin feat/11-issue-template`
6. Create PR with `gh pr create --base main --title "feat: customizable issue template" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Add `title_prefix` to customize issue title prefix (default: "[Radar]")
- Add `include_fields` to control which metadata rows appear in issue body
- Add `suggested_entry_format` with `{{var}}` interpolation for entry templates
- Backward compatible defaults

Closes #11

## Test plan
- [x] Custom title_prefix works
- [x] include_fields limits displayed metadata
- [x] suggested_entry_format renders with candidate data
- [x] Default behavior unchanged
- [x] All existing tests pass
- [x] Type check and build pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"`

{"parentUuid":"2f42a0c3-c976-4ebf-ab68-9c7ecc09bff1","isSidechain":true,"userType":"external","cwd":"/Users/moventsai/Projects/mine/awesome-list-radar","sessionId":"199cdb89-c906-4be2-b6ee-cb0e5cfc91c3","version":"2.1.69","gitBranch":"main","agentId":"a04d1fe1ffbfe2fdd","slug":"fluffy-wandering-flask","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import * as core from "@actions/core";
     2→import Parser from "rss-parser";
     3→import type { RadarConfig } from "../config";
     4→import type { Candidate } from "./types";
     5→
     6→const MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH = 1000;
     7→
     8→function matchesKeywords(text: string, keywords: string[]): boolean {
     9→  const lower = text.toLowerCase();
    10→  return keywords.some((kw) => lower.includes(kw.toLowerCase()));
    11→}
    12→
    13→export async function collectBlogs(
    14→  config: RadarConfig,
    15→  parser?: Parser
    16→): Promise<Candidate[]> {
    17→  if (!config.sources.blogs) return [];
    18→
    19→  const rssParser = parser ?? new Parser();
    20→  const blogs = config.sources.blogs;
    21→
    22→  const results = await Promise.allSettled(
    23→    blogs.feeds.map(async (feedUrl) => {
    24→      core.info(`Fetching feed: ${feedUrl}`);
    25→      return { feedUrl, feed: await rssParser.parseURL(feedUrl) };
    26→    })
    27→  );
    28→
    29→  const candidates: Candidate[] = [];
    30→
    31→  for (const result of results) {
    32→    if (result.status === "rejected") {
    33→      core.warning(`Failed to fetch feed: ${result.reason}`);
    34→      continue;
    35→    }
    36→
    37→    const { feed } = result.value;
    38→
    39→    for (const item of feed.items) {
    40→      if (!item.link || !item.title) continue;
    41→
    42→      const text = `${item.title} ${item.contentSnippet ?? item.content ?? ""}`;
    43→
    44→      if (blogs.keywords && blogs.keywords.length > 0) {
    45→        if (!matchesKeywords(text, blogs.keywords)) continue;
    46→      }
    47→
    48→      candidates.push({
    49→        url: item.link,
    50→        title: item.title,
    51→        description: (item.contentSnippet ?? item.content ?? "").slice(
    52→          0,
    53→          MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH
    54→        ),
    55→        source: "blog",
    56→        metadata: {
    57→          authors: item.creator ? [item.creator] : undefined,
    58→          publishedAt: item.isoDate ?? item.pubDate,
    59→          feedName: feed.title,
    60→        },
    61→      });
    62→    }
    63→  }
    64→
    65→  return candidates;
    66→}
    67→
    68→export { matchesKeywords };
    69→"}]},"uuid":"8e8c6860-6465-42f6-9460-289cb5da3e03","timestamp":"2026-03-05T09:37:31.753Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"2f42a0c3-c976-4ebf-ab68-9c7ecc09bff1"}

## Comment 1: Filter NOASSERTION spdx_id
File: `src/sources/github.ts`
Issue: When GitHub can't identify a license, API returns `spdx_id: "NOASSERTION"`. This should be filtered out.
Fix: Change `license: repo.license?.spdx_id` to:
```ts
license: repo.license?.spdx_id && repo.license.spdx_id !== "NOASSERTION" ? repo.license.spdx_id : undefined,
```

## Comment 2: Rename lastCommitAt to lastPushedAt
File: Multiple files
Issue: `repo.pushed_at` is not actually the last commit timestamp - it's updated by any push event. The field name is misleading.
Fix: Rename `lastCommitAt` to `lastPushedAt` across all files:
- `src/sources/types.ts`: rename the field in CandidateMetadata
- `src/sources/github.ts`: update the field name in the metadata object
- `src/classifier/llm.ts`: update the XML tag from `candidate_last_commit` to `candidate_last_pushed` and the field reference
- `src/output/issues.ts`: update the table row label from "Last Commit" to "Last Pushed" and field reference
- Update all test files that reference `lastCommitAt` to `lastPushedAt`

## Comment 3: Add assertions for second candidate
File: `tests/sources/github.test.ts`
Issue: Second mock repo has `archived: false` and `pushed_at` but no assertions for those fields.
Fix: Add assertions after `expect(candidates[1].metadata.language).toBeUndefined();`:
```ts
expect(candidates[1].metadata.license).toBeUndefined();
expect(candidates[1].metadata.homepage).toBeUndefined();
expect(candidates[1].metadata.fork).toBe(true);
expect(candidates[1].metadata.owner).toBe("test");
expect(candidates[1].metadata.archived).toBe(false);
expect(candidates[1].metadata.lastPushedAt).toBe("2025-01-10T00:00:00Z");
```
(Note: use `lastPushedAt` since we're renaming the field)

After making all changes:

5. Run `git push`

input.prompt:
You are working in the worktree at `/Users/moventsai/Projects/mine/awesome-list-radar/.claude/worktrees/agent-a7f40194` on branch `feat/7-github-pagination`.

Address these 2 Greptile code review comments:

## Comment 1: Over-fetching on last partial page
File: `src/sources/github.ts`
Issue: `perPage` is computed once as `Math.min(maxResults, 100)` and never adjusted. On the last page, unnecessary items are fetched and discarded.
Fix: Compute perPage dynamically inside the loop:
```ts
while (fetched < maxResults) {
  const remaining = maxResults - fetched;
  const perPage = Math.min(remaining, 100);
  const response = await client.search.repos({
    q: query,
    sort: gh.sort === "best-match" ? undefined : gh.sort,
    order: "desc",
    per_page: perPage,
    page,
  });
```
Remove the `const perPage = ...` line from before the loop.

## Comment 2: Unnecessary API call at exact page boundary
File: `src/sources/github.ts`
Issue: When total results is exact multiple of perPage, an extra empty API call is made.
Fix: After the for-loop inside the while, add a check using `total_count`:
```ts
if (response.data.items.length < perPage || fetched >= response.data.total_count) break;
page++;
```
(Replace the existing break condition and page increment with this)

After making all changes:
1. Run `bun run lint` and fix any issues
2. Run `bun test` and fix any issues
3. Run `bun run build`
4. Commit with message: `fix: optimize pagination - dynamic perPage and total_count guard`
5. Run `git push`

## Comment: No format validation on date_range strings
File: `src/config.ts`
Issue: ArXiv API expects dates in `YYYYMMDD` or `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS` format. Currently any string is accepted, so ISO format like `"2024-01-01"` silently produces malformed queries.
Fix: Add regex validation and a cross-field check. Change the `date_range` schema from:
```ts
date_range: z.object({ start: z.string(), end: z.string() }).optional(),
```
To:
```ts
date_range: z
  .object({
    start: z.string().regex(/^\d{8}(\d{6})?$/, 'Must be YYYYMMDD or YYYYMMDDHHMMSS'),
    end: z.string().regex(/^\d{8}(\d{6})?$/, 'Must be YYYYMMDD or YYYYMMDDHHMMSS'),
  })
  .refine((r) => r.start <= r.end, { message: "date_range.start must be <= date_range.end" })
  .optional(),
```

Also update any tests that use date_range to use the correct format (YYYYMMDD not ISO dates). Check `tests/sources/arxiv.test.ts` and `tests/config.test.ts` for any date_range test values.

After making all changes:
1. Run `bun run lint` and fix any issues
2. Run `bun test` and fix any issues
3. Run `bun run build`
4. Commit with message: `fix: validate ArXiv date_range format (YYYYMMDD)`
5. Run `git push`

input.description: Check CI failure logs for PR #25

content.text:
The CI failure is the `dist/` freshness check — the `dist/index.js` built locally differs from what's in the branch because `ncc` generates non-deterministic module IDs across different environments. The agents ran `bun run build` and committed `dist/`, but CI rebuilds and sees different chunk IDs.

Let me check how CI is configured and fix this.

content.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.head_ref || github.run_id }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'

      - run: npm ci

      - name: Type check
        run: npm run lint

      - name: Test
        run: npm test

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

      - name: Verify dist/ is up to date
        run: |
          git diff --exit-code dist/ || (echo "dist/ is out of date. Run 'npm run build' and commit." && exit 1)

content.thinking:
The issue is that CI uses `npm ci` and `npm run build` while the agents used `bun run build`. The different package managers result in different `ncc` output due to non-deterministic module IDs. The dist/ built locally with bun doesn't match what CI builds with npm.

The fix: I need to rebuild dist/ in each worktree using `npm` instead of `bun`, or better yet, rebuild using the same environment. But actually, the issue is that ncc generates different module chunk IDs based on the platform/environment. 

Let me think about the best approach. The CI uses `npm ci` then `npm run build`. 

The agents used `bun run build`, which is why the ncc bundler generated different internal module IDs. The "Verify dist/ is up to date" check fails because CI rebuilds with npm and gets different output than what was committed. The simplest solution is to rebuild dist/ in each of the 9 open PR branches using `npm ci && npm run build` to match CI's environment, then commit and push those changes.

for dir in agent-a7f40194 agent-adefdbe8 agent-a4453357 agent-a0fbbae4 agent-a98e9a58 agent-a3b44974 agent-ac8458d4 agent-a9ec4326 agent-a4b467f3; do

  npm run build 2>&1 | tail -1
  git add dist/
  if git diff --cached --quiet dist/; then
    echo "dist/ unchanged"
  else
    git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore: rebuild dist/ with npm for CI compatibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | head -1
    git push 2>&1 | tail -1
  fi
  echo
done

content.text:
No runs in progress or queued. The new pushes didn't trigger CI. This is likely because the CI has `concurrency: cancel-in-progress: true` and 9 pushes in quick succession caused all runs to cancel each other. Let me trigger them manually by pushing an empty commit on each branch, but space them out.

Actually, it could also be that the PR checks are stale. Let me just check if the PR head SHAs match the latest pushes.

input.command:
for pr in 17 18 19 20 21 22 23 24 25; do
  sha=$(gh pr view $pr --repo moven0831/awesome-list-radar --json headRefOid --jq '.headRefOid' 2>/dev/null)
  echo "PR #$pr: $sha"
done

for dir_branch in "agent-a7f40194:feat/7-github-pagination" "agent-adefdbe8:feat/8-source-configurability" "agent-a4453357:feat/9-filter-system" "agent-a0fbbae4:feat/10-smart-classification" "agent-a98e9a58:feat/11-issue-template" "agent-a3b44974:feat/12-registry-sources" "agent-ac8458d4:feat/13-retry-utility" "agent-a9ec4326:feat/14-incremental-state" "agent-a4b467f3:feat/15-cost-control"; do
  dir="${dir_branch%%:*}"
  branch="${dir_branch##*:}"
  sha=$(cd /Users/moventsai/Projects/mine/awesome-list-radar/.claude/worktrees/$dir && git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null)
  echo "$branch: $sha"
done

The new pushes aren't triggering CI runs. The empty commits were pushed directly, but CI only runs on `pull_request` events targeting `main`. The empty commits pushed to the feature branches should trigger the `pull_request` workflow since the PRs are open targeting main. But it seems they're not triggering.

Wait - looking at the CI workflow, it triggers on:
```yaml
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
```

So it only triggers on push to `main` or pull_request events against `main`. The push to feature branches should trigger the PR check... unless GitHub is throttling/rate limiting.

    33→After merging each wave, later-wave PRs rebase onto updated `main` before merging.
    34→
    35→## Phase 1: Parallel Implementation (10 worktree agents)
    36→
    37→Launch all 10 agents simultaneously, each with `isolation: "worktree"` and `run_in_background: true`.
    38→
    39→### Shared Worker Instructions
    40→
    41→Each worker receives:
    42→1. Project context (structure, conventions, commands)
    43→2. Issue-specific scope boundary and implementation steps
    44→3. Post-implementation checklist:
    45→   - `bun run lint` (type check)
    46→   - `bun test` (all tests pass)
    47→   - `bun run build` (ncc bundle)
    48→   - Commit: `feat: <description> (closes #N)`
    49→   - `git push -u origin <branch>`
    50→   - `gh pr create --title "<title>" --body "<description>"`
    51→
    52→### Issue-Specific Scopes
    53→
    54→**#6 (Metadata):** Add `license`, `archived`, `fork`, `owner`, `homepage`, `lastCommitAt` to `CandidateMetadata` in types.ts. Populate from GitHub API in github.ts. Add XML tags in llm.ts `buildUserPrompt()`. Add table rows in issues.ts `buildIssueBody()`. Tests: github.test.ts, llm.test.ts, issues.test.ts.
    55→
    56→**#7 (GitHub pagination):** Add `max_results` (max 1000), `sort`, `exclude_forks`, `exclude_archived` to `GithubSourceSchema`. Implement pagination loop in github.ts. Append `fork:false`/`archived:false` to query. Tests: github.test.ts, config.test.ts.
    57→
    58→**#8 (Source config):** ArXiv: configurable `max_results`, optional `date_range`. Web pages: configurable `extraction_prompt`, `model`, `request_timeout`, `user_agent`. Tests: arxiv.test.ts, web_pages.test.ts, config.test.ts.
    59→
    60→**#9 (Filter system):** New `FilterSchema` with `include`/`require_all`/`exclude` keywords + metadata filters (`exclude_forks`, `exclude_archived`, `require_license`, `max_age_days`). Enhanced URL normalization in dedup.ts. New `src/filter/metadata.ts`. Tests: keywords.test.ts, dedup.test.ts, new metadata.test.ts.
    61→
    62→**#10 (Classification):** Add `system_prompt`, `context`, `max_description_length`, `categories[]` to ClassificationSchema. New `src/utils/parse_list.ts` to extract ##/### headers from list_file. Include category tree in LLM prompt. Tests: llm.test.ts, new parse_list.test.ts.
    63→
    64→**#11 (Issue template):** Add `title_prefix`, `include_fields`, `suggested_entry_format` to IssueTemplateSchema. Implement `{{var}}` interpolation. Pass config to `buildIssueTitle`/`buildIssueBody`. Tests: issues.test.ts.
    65→
    66→**#12 (Registry):** New `src/sources/registry.ts` with npm/PyPI/crates.io collectors. Add `"registry"` to source union in types.ts. New `RegistrySourceSchema` in config.ts. Wire up in index.ts. Tests: new registry.test.ts.
    67→
    68→**#13 (Retry):** New `src/utils/retry.ts` with `withRetry()` (exponential backoff + jitter + rate limit detection). Wrap API calls in github.ts, arxiv.ts, blogs.ts, web_pages.ts, llm.ts, issues.ts. Tests: new retry.test.ts.
    69→
    70→**#14 (State):** New `src/state.ts` with `loadState`/`saveState`/`filterSeenCandidates`/`recordCandidates`. State file: `.radar-state.json`. Add `state_file` to config. Integrate in index.ts. Tests: new state.test.ts.
    71→
    72→**#15 (Cost):** Track `message.usage.input_tokens`/`output_tokens` from Anthropic API. Log per-candidate + cumulative cost. Add `max_budget_usd`, `max_classifications_per_run` to ClassificationSchema. Keep `max_issues_per_run` as deprecated alias. Tests: llm.test.ts.
    73→
    74→## Phase 2: Code Review (greptile)
    75→
    76→After each PR is created:
    77→1. Trigger greptile code review: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__trigger_code_review`
    78→2. Poll for completion: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__get_code_review`
    79→3. Read comments: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__list_merge_request_comments`
    80→4. Resume the worker agent to address feedback (push fix commits)
    81→5. Re-verify: `bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`
    82→
    83→## Phase 3: Merge (wave order)
    84→
    85→For each wave:
    86→1. Squash-merge PRs in wave using `mcp__plugin_github_github__merge_pull_request` with `merge_method: "squash"`
    87→2. After wave merges, later-wave PR branches need conflict resolution (rebase or merge main into branch)
    88→3. Re-run CI on updated branches before merging next wave
    89→
    90→## File Conflict Map
    91→
    92→| File | Issues | Resolution |
    93→|------|--------|------------|
    94→| `config.ts` | All 10 | Each adds distinct schema section. Textual conflicts at merge. |
    95→| `types.ts` | #6, #12 | #6 adds metadata fields, #12 adds source type. Orthogonal. |
    96→| `github.ts` | #6, #7, #13 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 2: #7. Sequential. |
    97→| `llm.ts` | #6, #10, #13, #15 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 2: #15, Wave 3: #10. Sequential. |
    98→| `issues.ts` | #6, #11, #13 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 3: #11. Sequential. |
    99→| `index.ts` | #12, #14 | Wave 4: both. Orthogonal additions. |

# Execution Plan: 10 Issues as Parallel PRs

## Context

We have 10 GitHub issues (#6-#15) for awesome-list-radar. Each needs: branch, implementation, tests, PR, greptile code review, feedback fixes, squash-merge. All 10 can be implemented in parallel via git worktrees since each branches from `main`. Merge conflicts (primarily in `config.ts`) are resolved at merge time in wave order.

## Issue-to-GitHub# Mapping

| Plan# | GH# | Title | Size | Branch |
|-------|------|-------|------|--------|
| 1 | #6 | Extend CandidateMetadata | S | `feat/6-extend-metadata` |
| 2 | #7 | GitHub pagination | M | `feat/7-github-pagination` |
| 3 | #8 | ArXiv + Web Pages config | S | `feat/8-source-configurability` |
| 4 | #9 | Filter system | M | `feat/9-filter-system` |
| 5 | #10 | Smart classification | M | `feat/10-smart-classification` |
| 6 | #11 | Custom issue template | M | `feat/11-issue-template` |
| 7 | #12 | Registry sources | L | `feat/12-registry-sources` |
| 8 | #13 | Retry utility | S | `feat/13-retry-utility` |
| 9 | #14 | Incremental state | M | `feat/14-incremental-state` |
| 10 | #15 | Cost control | S | `feat/15-cost-control` |

## Merge Order (Waves)

Config.ts is touched by all 10 issues. `llm.ts` by 4 issues. Wave ordering prevents semantic conflicts.

| Wave | PRs | Rationale |
|------|-----|-----------|
| 1 | #6, #13 | #6 adds types others consume. #13 is additive (new file + wrapping). |
| 2 | #7, #8, #15 | Extend source configs + cost control. Independent schema sections. |
| 3 | #9, #10, #11 | Filter, classification, output. Use metadata fields from #6. |
| 4 | #12, #14 | Registry (new source) + state (orchestration). Most independent. |

After merging each wave, later-wave PRs rebase onto updated `main` before merging.

## Phase 1: Parallel Implementation (10 worktree agents)

Launch all 10 agents simultaneously, each with `isolation: "worktree"` and `run_in_background: true`.

### Shared Worker Instructions

Each worker receives:
1. Project context (structure, conventions, commands)
2. Issue-specific scope boundary and implementation steps
3. Post-implementation checklist:
   - `bun run lint` (type check)
   - `bun test` (all tests pass)
   - `bun run build` (ncc bundle)
   - Commit: `feat: <description> (closes #N)`
   - `git push -u origin <branch>`
   - `gh pr create --title "<title>" --body "<description>"`

### Issue-Specific Scopes

**#6 (Metadata):** Add `license`, `archived`, `fork`, `owner`, `homepage`, `lastCommitAt` to `CandidateMetadata` in types.ts. Populate from GitHub API in github.ts. Add XML tags in llm.ts `buildUserPrompt()`. Add table rows in issues.ts `buildIssueBody()`. Tests: github.test.ts, llm.test.ts, issues.test.ts.

**#7 (GitHub pagination):** Add `max_results` (max 1000), `sort`, `exclude_forks`, `exclude_archived` to `GithubSourceSchema`. Implement pagination loop in github.ts. Append `fork:false`/`archived:false` to query. Tests: github.test.ts, config.test.ts.

**#8 (Source config):** ArXiv: configurable `max_results`, optional `date_range`. Web pages: configurable `extraction_prompt`, `model`, `request_timeout`, `user_agent`. Tests: arxiv.test.ts, web_pages.test.ts, config.test.ts.

**#9 (Filter system):** New `FilterSchema` with `include`/`require_all`/`exclude` keywords + metadata filters (`exclude_forks`, `exclude_archived`, `require_license`, `max_age_days`). Enhanced URL normalization in dedup.ts. New `src/filter/metadata.ts`. Tests: keywords.test.ts, dedup.test.ts, new metadata.test.ts.

**#10 (Classification):** Add `system_prompt`, `context`, `max_description_length`, `categories[]` to ClassificationSchema. New `src/utils/parse_list.ts` to extract ##/### headers from list_file. Include category tree in LLM prompt. Tests: llm.test.ts, new parse_list.test.ts.

**#11 (Issue template):** Add `title_prefix`, `include_fields`, `suggested_entry_format` to IssueTemplateSchema. Implement `{{var}}` interpolation. Pass config to `buildIssueTitle`/`buildIssueBody`. Tests: issues.test.ts.

**#12 (Registry):** New `src/sources/registry.ts` with npm/PyPI/crates.io collectors. Add `"registry"` to source union in types.ts. New `RegistrySourceSchema` in config.ts. Wire up in index.ts. Tests: new registry.test.ts.

**#13 (Retry):** New `src/utils/retry.ts` with `withRetry()` (exponential backoff + jitter + rate limit detection). Wrap API calls in github.ts, arxiv.ts, blogs.ts, web_pages.ts, llm.ts, issues.ts. Tests: new retry.test.ts.

**#14 (State):** New `src/state.ts` with `loadState`/`saveState`/`filterSeenCandidates`/`recordCandidates`. State file: `.radar-state.json`. Add `state_file` to config. Integrate in index.ts. Tests: new state.test.ts.

**#15 (Cost):** Track `message.usage.input_tokens`/`output_tokens` from Anthropic API. Log per-candidate + cumulative cost. Add `max_budget_usd`, `max_classifications_per_run` to ClassificationSchema. Keep `max_issues_per_run` as deprecated alias. Tests: llm.test.ts.

## Phase 2: Code Review (greptile)

After each PR is created:
1. Trigger greptile code review: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__trigger_code_review`
2. Poll for completion: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__get_code_review`
3. Read comments: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__list_merge_request_comments`
4. Resume the worker agent to address feedback (push fix commits)
5. Re-verify: `bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`

## Phase 3: Merge (wave order)

For each wave:
1. Squash-merge PRs in wave using `mcp__plugin_github_github__merge_pull_request` with `merge_method: "squash"`
2. After wave merges, later-wave PR branches need conflict resolution (rebase or merge main into branch)
3. Re-run CI on updated branches before merging next wave

## File Conflict Map

| File | Issues | Resolution |
|------|--------|------------|
| `config.ts` | All 10 | Each adds distinct schema section. Textual conflicts at merge. |
| `types.ts` | #6, #12 | #6 adds metadata fields, #12 adds source type. Orthogonal. |
| `github.ts` | #6, #7, #13 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 2: #7. Sequential. |
| `llm.ts` | #6, #10, #13, #15 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 2: #15, Wave 3: #10. Sequential. |
| `issues.ts` | #6, #11, #13 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 3: #11. Sequential. |
| `index.ts` | #12, #14 | Wave 4: both. Orthogonal additions. |

## Verification

Per-PR: `bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`
Per-wave (after merge to main): `bun install && bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`
Final: All 84+ original tests pass + new tests for all 10 features.
