# Execution Plan: 10 Issues as Parallel PRs

## Context

We have 10 GitHub issues (#6-#15) for awesome-list-radar. Each needs: branch, implementation, tests, PR, greptile code review, feedback fixes, squash-merge. All 10 can be implemented in parallel via git worktrees since each branches from `main`. Merge conflicts (primarily in `config.ts`) are resolved at merge time in wave order.

## Issue-to-GitHub# Mapping

| Plan# | GH# | Title | Size | Branch |
|-------|------|-------|------|--------|
| 1 | #6 | Extend CandidateMetadata | S | `feat/6-extend-metadata` |
| 2 | #7 | GitHub pagination | M | `feat/7-github-pagination` |
| 3 | #8 | ArXiv + Web Pages config | S | `feat/8-source-configurability` |
| 4 | #9 | Filter system | M | `feat/9-filter-system` |
| 5 | #10 | Smart classification | M | `feat/10-smart-classification` |
| 6 | #11 | Custom issue template | M | `feat/11-issue-template` |
| 7 | #12 | Registry sources | L | `feat/12-registry-sources` |
| 8 | #13 | Retry utility | S | `feat/13-retry-utility` |
| 9 | #14 | Incremental state | M | `feat/14-incremental-state` |
| 10 | #15 | Cost control | S | `feat/15-cost-control` |

## Merge Order (Waves)

Config.ts is touched by all 10 issues. `llm.ts` by 4 issues. Wave ordering prevents semantic conflicts.

| Wave | PRs | Rationale |
|------|-----|-----------|
| 1 | #6, #13 | #6 adds types others consume. #13 is additive (new file + wrapping). |
| 2 | #7, #8, #15 | Extend source configs + cost control. Independent schema sections. |
| 3 | #9, #10, #11 | Filter, classification, output. Use metadata fields from #6. |
| 4 | #12, #14 | Registry (new source) + state (orchestration). Most independent. |

After merging each wave, later-wave PRs rebase onto updated `main` before merging.

## Phase 1: Parallel Implementation (10 worktree agents)

Launch all 10 agents simultaneously, each with `isolation: "worktree"` and `run_in_background: true`.

### Shared Worker Instructions

Each worker receives:
1. Project context (structure, conventions, commands)
2. Issue-specific scope boundary and implementation steps
3. Post-implementation checklist:
   - `bun run lint` (type check)
   - `bun test` (all tests pass)
   - `bun run build` (ncc bundle)
   - Commit: `feat: <description> (closes #N)`
   - `git push -u origin <branch>`
   - `gh pr create --title "<title>" --body "<description>"`

### Issue-Specific Scopes

**#6 (Metadata):** Add `license`, `archived`, `fork`, `owner`, `homepage`, `lastCommitAt` to `CandidateMetadata` in types.ts. Populate from GitHub API in github.ts. Add XML tags in llm.ts `buildUserPrompt()`. Add table rows in issues.ts `buildIssueBody()`. Tests: github.test.ts, llm.test.ts, issues.test.ts.

**#7 (GitHub pagination):** Add `max_results` (max 1000), `sort`, `exclude_forks`, `exclude_archived` to `GithubSourceSchema`. Implement pagination loop in github.ts. Append `fork:false`/`archived:false` to query. Tests: github.test.ts, config.test.ts.

**#8 (Source config):** ArXiv: configurable `max_results`, optional `date_range`. Web pages: configurable `extraction_prompt`, `model`, `request_timeout`, `user_agent`. Tests: arxiv.test.ts, web_pages.test.ts, config.test.ts.

**#9 (Filter system):** New `FilterSchema` with `include`/`require_all`/`exclude` keywords + metadata filters (`exclude_forks`, `exclude_archived`, `require_license`, `max_age_days`). Enhanced URL normalization in dedup.ts. New `src/filter/metadata.ts`. Tests: keywords.test.ts, dedup.test.ts, new metadata.test.ts.

**#10 (Classification):** Add `system_prompt`, `context`, `max_description_length`, `categories[]` to ClassificationSchema. New `src/utils/parse_list.ts` to extract ##/### headers from list_file. Include category tree in LLM prompt. Tests: llm.test.ts, new parse_list.test.ts.

**#11 (Issue template):** Add `title_prefix`, `include_fields`, `suggested_entry_format` to IssueTemplateSchema. Implement `{{var}}` interpolation. Pass config to `buildIssueTitle`/`buildIssueBody`. Tests: issues.test.ts.

**#12 (Registry):** New `src/sources/registry.ts` with npm/PyPI/crates.io collectors. Add `"registry"` to source union in types.ts. New `src/sources/types.ts`. New `RegistrySourceSchema` in config.ts. Wire up in index.ts. Tests: new registry.test.ts.

**#13 (Retry):** New `src/utils/retry.ts` with `withRetry()` (exponential backoff + jitter + rate limit detection). Wrap API calls in github.ts, arxiv.ts, blogs.ts, web_pages.ts, llm.ts, issues.ts. Tests: new retry.test.ts.

**#14 (State):** New `src/state.ts` with `loadState`/`saveState`/`filterSeenCandidates`/`recordCandidates`. State file: `.radar-state.json`. Add `state_file` to config. Integrate in index.ts. Tests: new state.test.ts.

**#15 (Cost):** Track `message.usage.input_tokens`/`output_tokens` from Anthropic API. Log per-candidate + cumulative cost. Add `max_budget_usd`, `max_classifications_per_run` to ClassificationSchema. Keep `max_issues_per_run` as deprecated alias. Tests: llm.test.ts.

## Phase 2: Code Review (greptile)

After each PR is created:
1. Trigger greptile code review: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__trigger_code_review`
2. Poll for completion: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__get_code_review`
3. Read comments: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__list_merge_request_comments`
4. Resume the worker agent to address feedback (push fix commits)
5. Re-verify: `bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`

## Phase 3: Merge (wave order)

For each wave:
1. Squash-merge PRs in wave using `mcp__plugin_github_github__merge_pull_request` with `merge_method: "squash"`
2. After wave merges, later-wave PR branches need conflict resolution (rebase or merge main into branch)
3. Re-run CI on updated branches before merging next wave

## File Conflict Map

| File | Issues | Resolution |
|------|--------|------------|
| `config.ts` | All 10 | Each adds distinct schema section. Textual conflicts at merge. |
| `types.ts` | #6, #12 | #6 adds metadata fields, #12 adds source type. Orthogonal. |
| `github.ts` | #6, #7, #13 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 2: #7. Sequential. |
| `llm.ts` | #6, #10, #13, #15 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 2: #15, Wave 3: #10. Sequential. |
| `issues.ts` | #6, #11, #13 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 3: #11. Sequential. |
| `index.ts` | #12, #14 | Wave 4: both. Orthogonal additions. |

## Verification

Per-PR: `bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`
Per-wave (after merge to main): `bun install && bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`
Final: All 84+ original tests pass + new tests for all 10 features.

# Plan: Resolve Merge Conflicts and Continue Wave Merges

## Context

10 PRs (#16-#25) were implemented in parallel worktrees for issues #6-#15. PR #16 (Metadata) was merged to main. All 9 remaining PRs now have merge conflicts against updated main. The user wants conflicts resolved before continuing wave merges.

## Current State

- **Merged**: PR #16 (feat/6-extend-metadata) → main
- **Open PRs**: #17-#25 (9 PRs), all have merge conflicts
- **CI**: Failing on all PRs (dist/ freshness check + conflicts)

## Conflict Analysis

| PR | Branch | Worktree | Real Conflicts | Dist Only |
|----|--------|----------|----------------|-----------|
| #17 | feat/7-github-pagination | agent-a7f40194 | `src/sources/github.ts` | + dist/ |
| #18 | feat/8-source-configurability | agent-adefdbe8 | None | dist/ only |
| #19 | feat/9-filter-system | agent-a4453357 | `src/sources/types.ts` | + dist/ |
| #20 | feat/10-smart-classification | agent-a0fbbae4 | None | dist/ only |
| #21 | feat/11-issue-template | agent-a98e9a58 | None | dist/ only |
| #22 | feat/12-registry-sources | agent-a3b44974 | `src/sources/types.ts` | + dist/ |
| #23 | feat/13-retry-utility | agent-ac8458d4 | `github.ts`, `llm.ts`, `issues.ts` | + dist/ |
| #24 | feat/14-incremental-state | agent-a9ec4326 | None | dist/ only |
| #25 | feat/15-cost-control | agent-a4b467f3 | None | dist/ only |

## Plan: Resolve All 9 PRs

### Strategy
For each PR: merge `origin/main` into the branch, resolve conflicts, rebuild dist/ with `npm`, verify (lint+test+build), commit, push.

### Wave 1 Remaining: PR #23 (Retry)

**PR #23** (`src/sources/github.ts`, `src/classifier/llm.ts`, `src/output/issues.ts`):
- `github.ts`: Main now has new metadata fields from #6. PR #23 wraps the API call with `withRetry()`. Resolution: keep both changes — metadata fields + retry wrapper.
- `llm.ts`: Main now has new XML tags from #6. PR #23 wraps `anthropic.messages.create()` with `withRetry()`. Resolution: keep both.
- `issues.ts`: Main now has new table rows from #6. PR #23 wraps `listIssues`/`createIssue` with `withRetry()`. Resolution: keep both.

### Wave 2: PRs #17, #18, #25

**PR #17** (`src/sources/github.ts`):
- Main now has metadata fields from #6. PR #17 adds pagination loop. Resolution: the pagination loop should include the new metadata fields in the candidate push.

**PR #18**: dist/ only — just merge main and rebuild.

**PR #25**: dist/ only — just merge main and rebuild.

### Wave 3: PRs #19, #20, #21

**PR #19** (`src/sources/types.ts`):
- Main added `license`, `archived`, `fork`, `owner`, `homepage`, `lastPushedAt` to CandidateMetadata. PR #19 also added `fork`, `archived`, `license`, `lastCommitAt`. Resolution: take main's version (superset with `lastPushedAt` instead of `lastCommitAt`).

**PR #20**, **PR #21**: dist/ only — merge main and rebuild.

### Wave 4: PRs #22, #24

**PR #22** (`src/sources/types.ts`):
- Main added metadata fields. PR #22 added `"registry"` to source union. Resolution: keep both.

**PR #24**: dist/ only — merge main and rebuild.

### Execution Steps (per PR)

```bash
cd <worktree>
git fetch origin main
git merge origin/main
# Resolve conflicts in src/ files (keep both changes)
# For dist/ conflicts: just delete dist/ and rebuild
rm -rf dist/
npm run build
bun run lint && bun test
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: merge main and resolve conflicts"
git push
```

### Order of Conflict Resolution

Process in wave order to minimize re-resolving:
1. PR #23 (Wave 1 — retry, most conflicts, 3 source files)
2. PR #17 (Wave 2 — pagination, 1 source file)
3. PR #18 (Wave 2 — dist only)
4. PR #25 (Wave 2 — dist only)
5. PR #19 (Wave 3 — types.ts)
6. PR #20 (Wave 3 — dist only)
7. PR #21 (Wave 3 — dist only)
8. PR #22 (Wave 4 — types.ts)
9. PR #24 (Wave 4 — dist only)

## Verification

After each PR's conflicts are resolved:
1. `bun run lint` — type check passes
2. `bun test` — all tests pass
3. `npm run build` — dist/ rebuilt with npm (for CI compatibility)
4. Push and verify CI passes

## Files Reference

- Worktree base: `/Users/moventsai/Projects/mine/awesome-list-radar/.claude/worktrees/`
- Main `github.ts`: `/Users/moventsai/Projects/mine/awesome-list-radar/src/sources/github.ts`

**#10 (Classification):** Add `system_prompt`, `context`, `max_description_length`, `categories[]` to ClassificationSchema. New `src/utils/parse_list.ts` to extract ##/### headers from list_file. Include category tree in LLM prompt. Tests: llm.test.ts, new parse_list.test.ts.

**#11 (Issue template):** Add `title_prefix`, `include_fields`, `suggested_entry_format` to IssueTemplateSchema. Implement `{{var}}` interpolation. Pass config to `buildIssueTitle`/`buildIssueBody`. Tests: issues.test.ts.

**#12 (Registry):** New `src/sources/registry.ts` with npm/PyPI/crates.io collectors. Add `"registry"` to source union in types.ts. New `src/sources/types.ts`. New `RegistrySourceSchema` in config.ts. Wire up in index.ts. Tests: new registry.test.ts.

**#13 (Retry):** New `src/utils/retry.ts` with `withRetry()` (exponential backoff + jitter + rate limit detection). Wrap API calls in github.ts, arxiv.ts, blogs.ts, web_pages.ts, llm.ts, issues.ts. Tests: new retry.test.ts.

**#14 (State):** New `src/state.ts` with `loadState`/`saveState`/`filterSeenCandidates`/`recordCandidates`. State file: `.radar-state.json`. Add `state_file` to config. Integrate in index.ts. Tests: new state.test.ts.

**#15 (Cost):** Track `message.usage.input_tokens`/`output_tokens` from Anthropic API. Log per-candidate + cumulative cost. Add `max_budget_usd`, `max_classifications_per_run` to ClassificationSchema. Keep `max_issues_per_run` as deprecated alias. Tests: llm.test.ts.

## Phase 2: Code Review (greptile)

After each PR is created:
1. Trigger greptile code review: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__trigger_code_review`
2. Poll for completion: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__get_code_review`
3. Read comments: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__list_merge_request_comments`
4. Resume the worker agent to address feedback (push fix commits)
5. Re-verify: `bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`

## Phase 3: Merge (wave order)

For each wave:
1. Squash-merge PRs in wave using `mcp__plugin_github_github__merge_pull_request` with `merge_method: "squash"`
2. After wave merges, later-wave PR branches need conflict resolution (rebase or merge main into branch)
3. Re-run CI on updated branches before merging next wave

## File Conflict Map

| File | Issues | Resolution |
|------|--------|------------|
| `config.ts` | All 10 | Each adds distinct schema section. Textual conflicts at merge. |
| `types.ts` | #6, #12 | #6 adds metadata fields, #12 adds source type. Orthogonal. |
| `github.ts` | #6, #7, #13 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 2: #7. Sequential. |
| `llm.ts` | #6, #10, #13, #15 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 2: #15, Wave 3: #10. Sequential. |
| `issues.ts` | #6, #11, #13 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 3: #11. Sequential. |
| `index.ts` | #12, #14 | Wave 4: both. Orthogonal additions. |

## Verification

Per-PR: `bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`
Per-wave (after merge to main): `bun install && bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`
Final: All 84+ original tests pass + new tests for all 10 features.</tool_use_error>

String: # Execution Plan: 10 Issues as Parallel PRs

## Context

We have 10 GitHub issues (#6-#15) for awesome-list-radar. Each needs: branch, implementation, tests, PR, greptile code review, feedback fixes, squash-merge. All 10 can be implemented in parallel via git worktrees since each branches from `main`. Merge conflicts (primarily in `config.ts`) are resolved at merge time in wave order.

## Issue-to-GitHub# Mapping

| Plan# | GH# | Title | Size | Branch |
|-------|------|-------|------|--------|
| 1 | #6 | Extend CandidateMetadata | S | `feat/6-extend-metadata` |
| 2 | #7 | GitHub pagination | M | `feat/7-github-pagination` |
| 3 | #8 | ArXiv + Web Pages config | S | `feat/8-source-configurability` |
| 4 | #9 | Filter system | M | `feat/9-filter-system` |
| 5 | #10 | Smart classification | M | `feat/10-smart-classification` |
| 6 | #11 | Custom issue template | M | `feat/11-issue-template` |
| 7 | #12 | Registry sources | L | `feat/12-registry-sources` |
| 8 | #13 | Retry utility | S | `feat/13-retry-utility` |
| 9 | #14 | Incremental state | M | `feat/14-incremental-state` |
| 10 | #15 | Cost control | S | `feat/15-cost-control` |

## Merge Order (Waves)

Config.ts is touched by all 10 issues. `llm.ts` by 4 issues. Wave ordering prevents semantic conflicts.

| Wave | PRs | Rationale |
|------|-----|-----------|
| 1 | #6, #13 | #6 adds types others consume. #13 is additive (new file + wrapping). |
| 2 | #7, #8, #15 | Extend source configs + cost control. Independent schema sections. |
| 3 | #9, #10, #11 | Filter, classification, output. Use metadata fields from #6. |
| 4 | #12, #14 | Registry (new source) + state (orchestration). Most independent. |

After merging each wave, later-wave PRs rebase onto updated `main` before merging.

## Phase 1: Parallel Implementation (10 worktree agents)

Launch all 10 agents simultaneously, each with `isolation: "worktree"` and `run_in_background: true`.

### Shared Worker Instructions

Each worker receives:
1. Project context (structure, conventions, commands)
2. Issue-specific scope boundary and implementation steps
3. Post-implementation checklist:
   - `bun run lint` (type check)
   - `bun test` (all tests pass)
   - `bun run build` (ncc bundle)
   - Commit: `feat: <description> (closes #N)`
   - `git push -u origin <branch>`
   - `gh pr create --title "<title>" --body "<description>"`

### Issue-Specific Scopes

**#6 (Metadata):** Add `license`, `archived`, `fork`, `owner`, `homepage`, `lastCommitAt` to `CandidateMetadata` in types.ts. Populate from GitHub API in github.ts. Add XML tags in llm.ts `buildUserPrompt()`. Add table rows in issues.ts `buildIssueBody()`. Tests: github.test.ts, llm.test.ts, issues.test.ts.

**#7 (GitHub pagination):** Add `max_results` (max 1000), `sort`, `exclude_forks`, `exclude_archived` to `GithubSourceSchema`. Implement pagination loop in github.ts. Append `fork:false`/`archived:false` to query. Tests: github.test.ts, config.test.ts.

**#8 (Source config):** ArXiv: configurable `max_results`, optional `date_range`. Web pages: configurable `extraction_prompt`, `model`, `request_timeout`, `user_agent`. Tests: arxiv.test.ts, web_pages.test.ts, config.test.ts.

**#9 (Filter system):** New `FilterSchema` with `include`/`require_all`/`exclude` keywords + metadata filters (`exclude_forks`, `exclude_archived`, `require_license`, `max_age_days`). Enhanced URL normalization in dedup.ts. New `src/filter/metadata.ts`. Tests: keywords.test.ts, dedup.test.ts, new metadata.test.ts.

**#10 (Classification):** Add `system_prompt`, `context`, `max_description_length`, `categories[]` to ClassificationSchema. New `src/utils/parse_list.ts` to extract ##/### headers from list_file. Include category tree in LLM prompt. Tests: llm.test.ts, new parse_list.test.ts.

**#11 (Issue template):** Add `title_prefix`, `include_fields`, `suggested_entry_format` to IssueTemplateSchema. Implement `{{var}}` interpolation. Pass config to `buildIssueTitle`/`buildIssueBody`. Tests: issues.test.ts.

**#12 (Registry):** New `src/sources/registry.ts` with npm/PyPI/crates.io collectors. Add `"registry"` to source union in types.ts. New `src/sources/types.ts`. New `RegistrySourceSchema` in config.ts. Wire up in index.ts. Tests: new registry.test.ts.

**#13 (Retry):** New `src/utils/retry.ts` with `withRetry()` (exponential backoff + jitter + rate limit detection). Wrap API calls in github.ts, arxiv.ts, blogs.ts, web_pages.ts, llm.ts, issues.ts. Tests: new retry.test.ts.

**#14 (State):** New `src/state.ts` with `loadState`/`saveState`/`filterSeenCandidates`/`recordCandidates`. State file: `.radar-state.json`. Add `state_file` to config. Integrate in index.ts. Tests: new state.test.ts.

**#15 (Cost):** Track `message.usage.input_tokens`/`output_tokens` from Anthropic API. Log per-candidate + cumulative cost. Add `max_budget_usd`, `max_classifications_per_run` to ClassificationSchema. Keep `max_issues_per_run` as deprecated alias. Tests: llm.test.ts.

## Phase 2: Code Review (greptile)

After each PR is created:
1. Trigger greptile code review: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__trigger_code_review`
2. Poll for completion: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__get_code_review`
3. Read comments: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__list_merge_request_comments`
4. Resume the worker agent to address feedback (push fix commits)
5. Re-verify: `bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`

## Phase 3: Merge (wave order)

For each wave:
1. Squash-merge PRs in wave using `mcp__plugin_github_github__merge_pull_request` with `merge_method: "squash"`
2. After wave merges, later-wave PR branches need conflict resolution (rebase or merge main into branch)
3. Re-run CI on updated branches before merging next wave

## File Conflict Map

| File | Issues | Resolution |
|------|--------|------------|
| `config.ts` | All 10 | Each adds distinct schema section. Textual conflicts at merge. |
| `types.ts` | #6, #12 | #6 adds metadata fields, #12 adds source type. Orthogonal. |
| `github.ts` | #6, #7, #13 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 2: #7. Sequential. |
| `llm.ts` | #6, #10, #13, #15 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 2: #15, Wave 3: #10. Sequential. |
| `issues.ts` | #6, #11, #13 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 3: #11. Sequential. |
| `index.ts` | #12, #14 | Wave 4: both. Orthogonal additions. |

## Verification

Per-PR: `bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`
Per-wave (after merge to main): `bun install && bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`
Final: All 84+ original tests pass + new tests for all 10 features.

input.content:
# Plan: Resolve Merge Conflicts and Continue Wave Merges

## Context

10 PRs (#16-#25) were implemented in parallel worktrees for issues #6-#15. PR #16 (Metadata, issue #6) was squash-merged to main. All 9 remaining PRs now have merge conflicts against updated main. CI is also failing because `dist/` was built with `bun` but CI uses `npm` (different ncc chunk IDs).

## Current State

- **Merged**: PR #16 (feat/6-extend-metadata) → main (adds `license`, `archived`, `fork`, `owner`, `homepage`, `lastPushedAt` to metadata)
- **Open PRs**: #17-#25 (9 PRs), all with merge conflicts
- **CI**: Failing on all PRs (dist/ freshness + merge conflicts)

## Conflict Analysis

| PR | Worktree | Real Source Conflicts | Dist Only? |
|----|----------|----------------------|------------|
| #23 | agent-ac8458d4 | `github.ts`, `llm.ts`, `issues.ts` | No |
| #17 | agent-a7f40194 | `github.ts` | No |
| #19 | agent-a4453357 | `types.ts` | No |
| #22 | agent-a3b44974 | `types.ts` | No |
| #18 | agent-adefdbe8 | None | Yes |
| #20 | agent-a0fbbae4 | None | Yes |
| #21 | agent-a98e9a58 | None | Yes |
| #24 | agent-a9ec4326 | None | Yes |
| #25 | agent-a4b467f3 | None | Yes |

## Execution Plan

### For each of the 9 PRs (process in wave order):

```bash
cd /Users/moventsai/Projects/mine/awesome-list-radar/.claude/worktrees/<agent-dir>
git fetch origin main
git merge origin/main    # will report conflicts
```

Then resolve conflicts per-PR:

**Wave 1 remaining — PR #23 (Retry):**
- `github.ts`: Main added metadata fields (`license`, `archived`, etc.) in candidate push. PR #23 wraps `client.search.repos()` with `withRetry()`. Resolution: keep both — metadata fields AND retry wrapper.
- `llm.ts`: Main added XML tags for new metadata. PR #23 wraps `anthropic.messages.create()` with `withRetry()`. Resolution: keep both.
- `issues.ts`: Main added table rows for new metadata. PR #23 wraps `listIssues`/`createIssue` with `withRetry()`. Resolution: keep both.

**Wave 2 — PR #17 (Pagination):**
- `github.ts`: Main added metadata fields in single-page fetch. PR #17 replaces with pagination loop. Resolution: pagination loop must include the new metadata fields (`license`, `archived`, `fork`, `owner`, `homepage`, `lastPushedAt`) in candidate push inside the loop.

**Wave 2 — PRs #18, #25:** dist/ only — merge main, delete dist/, rebuild with `npm`.

**Wave 3 — PR #19 (Filter):**
- `types.ts`: Main added 6 fields. PR #19 also added `fork?`, `archived?`, `license?`, `lastCommitAt?`. Resolution: take main's version (superset; use `lastPushedAt` not `lastCommitAt`). Remove duplicates PR #19 added.

**Wave 3 — PRs #20, #21:** dist/ only — merge main, rebuild.

**Wave 4 — PR #22 (Registry):**
- `types.ts`: Main added metadata fields. PR #22 added `"registry"` to source union. Resolution: keep both changes (orthogonal).

**Wave 4 — PR #24:** dist/ only — merge main, rebuild.

### After conflict resolution for each PR:

```bash
# Resolve source conflicts manually
git add <resolved-files>
# Rebuild dist/ with npm (matches CI)
rm -rf dist/
npm ci && npm run build
# Verify
bun run lint && bun test
# Commit and push
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: merge main and resolve conflicts"
git push
```

### Then continue wave merges:

1. Squash-merge Wave 1 remaining (PR #23)
2. Merge main into Wave 2 PRs, resolve, squash-merge (#17, #18, #25)
3. Merge main into Wave 3 PRs, resolve, squash-merge (#19, #20, #21)
4. Merge main into Wave 4 PRs, resolve, squash-merge (#22, #24)

## Key Files

- Worktree base: `/Users/moventsai/Projects/mine/awesome-list-radar/.claude/worktrees/`
- Main `github.ts` (post-merge): has metadata fields + single-page fetch
- Main `types.ts` (post-merge): has `lastPushedAt`, `license`, `archived`, `fork`, `owner`, `homepage`

## Verification

Per-PR after conflict resolution:
1. `bun run lint` — type check passes
2. `bun test` — all tests pass
3. `npm run build` — dist/ built with npm for CI compat
4. Push → CI should pass

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Execution Plan: 10 Issues as Parallel PRs

## Context

We have 10 GitHub issues (#6-#15) for awesome-list-radar. Each needs: branch, implementation, tests, PR, greptile code review, feedback fixes, squash-merge. All 10 can be implemented in parallel via git worktrees since each branches from `main`. Merge conflicts (primarily in `config.ts`) are resolved at merge time in wave order.

## Issue-to-GitHub# Mapping

| Plan# | GH# | Title | Size | Branch |
|-------|------|-------|------|--------|
| 1 | #6 | Extend CandidateMetadata | S | `feat/6-extend-metadata` |
| 2 | #7 | GitHub pagination | M | `feat/7-github-pagination` |
| 3 | #8 | ArXiv + Web Pages config | S | `feat/8-source-configurability` |
| 4 | #9 | Filter system | M | `feat/9-filter-system` |
| 5 | #10 | Smart classification | M | `feat/10-smart-classification` |
| 6 | #11 | Custom issue template | M | `feat/11-issue-template` |
| 7 | #12 | Registry sources | L | `feat/12-registry-sources` |
| 8 | #13 | Retry utility | S | `feat/13-retry-utility` |
| 9 | #14 | Incremental state | M | `feat/14-incremental-state` |
| 10 | #15 | Cost control | S | `feat/15-cost-control` |

## Merge Order (Waves)

Config.ts is touched by all 10 issues. `llm.ts` by 4 issues. Wave ordering prevents semantic conflicts.

| Wave | PRs | Rationale |
|------|-----|-----------|
| 1 | #6, #13 | #6 adds types others consume. #13 is additive (new file + wrapping). |
| 2 | #7, #8, #15 | Extend source configs + cost control. Independent schema sections. |
| 3 | #9, #10, #11 | Filter, classification, output. Use metadata fields from #6. |
| 4 | #12, #14 | Registry (new source) + state (orchestration). Most independent. |

After merging each wave, later-wave PRs rebase onto updated `main` before merging.

## Phase 1: Parallel Implementation (10 worktree agents)

Launch all 10 agents simultaneously, each with `isolation: "worktree"` and `run_in_background: true`.

### Shared Worker Instructions

Each worker receives:
1. Project context (structure, conventions, commands)
2. Issue-specific scope boundary and implementation steps
3. Post-implementation checklist:
   - `bun run lint` (type check)
   - `bun test` (all tests pass)
   - `bun run build` (ncc bundle)
   - Commit: `feat: <description> (closes #N)`
   - `git push -u origin <branch>`
   - `gh pr create --title "<title>" --body "<description>"`

### Issue-Specific Scopes

**#6 (Metadata):** Add `license`, `archived`, `fork`, `owner`, `homepage`, `lastCommitAt` to `CandidateMetadata` in types.ts. Populate from GitHub API in github.ts. Add XML tags in llm.ts `buildUserPrompt()`. Add table rows in issues.ts `buildIssueBody()`. Tests: github.test.ts, llm.test.ts, issues.test.ts.

**#7 (GitHub pagination):** Add `max_results` (max 1000), `sort`, `exclude_forks`, `exclude_archived` to `GithubSourceSchema`. Implement pagination loop in github.ts. Append `fork:false`/`archived:false` to query. Tests: github.test.ts, config.test.ts.

**#8 (Source config):** ArXiv: configurable `max_results`, optional `date_range`. Web pages: configurable `extraction_prompt`, `model`, `request_timeout`, `user_agent`. Tests: arxiv.test.ts, web_pages.test.ts, config.test.ts.

**#9 (Filter system):** New `FilterSchema` with `include`/`require_all`/`exclude` keywords + metadata filters (`exclude_forks`, `exclude_archived`, `require_license`, `max_age_days`). Enhanced URL normalization in dedup.ts. New `src/filter/metadata.ts`. Tests: keywords.test.ts, dedup.test.ts, new metadata.test.ts.

**#10 (Classification):** Add `system_prompt`, `context`, `max_description_length`, `categories[]` to ClassificationSchema. New `src/utils/parse_list.ts` to extract ##/### headers from list_file. Include category tree in LLM prompt. Tests: llm.test.ts, new parse_list.test.ts.

**#11 (Issue template):** Add `title_prefix`, `include_fields`, `suggested_entry_format` to IssueTemplateSchema. Implement `{{var}}` interpolation. Pass config to `buildIssueTitle`/`buildIssueBody`. Tests: issues.test.ts.

**#12 (Registry):** New `src/sources/registry.ts` with npm/PyPI/crates.io collectors. Add `"registry"` to source union in types.ts. New `RegistrySourceSchema` in config.ts. Wire up in index.ts. Tests: new registry.test.ts.

**#13 (Retry):** New `src/utils/retry.ts` with `withRetry()` (exponential backoff + jitter + rate limit detection). Wrap API calls in github.ts, arxiv.ts, blogs.ts, web_pages.ts, llm.ts, issues.ts. Tests: new retry.test.ts.

**#14 (State):** New `src/state.ts` with `loadState`/`saveState`/`filterSeenCandidates`/`recordCandidates`. State file: `.radar-state.json`. Add `state_file` to config. Integrate in index.ts. Tests: new state.test.ts.

**#15 (Cost):** Track `message.usage.input_tokens`/`output_tokens` from Anthropic API. Log per-candidate + cumulative cost. Add `max_budget_usd`, `max_classifications_per_run` to ClassificationSchema. Keep `max_issues_per_run` as deprecated alias. Tests: llm.test.ts.

## Phase 2: Code Review (greptile)

After each PR is created:
1. Trigger greptile code review: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__trigger_code_review`
2. Poll for completion: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__get_code_review`
3. Read comments: `mcp__plugin_greptile_greptile__list_merge_request_comments`
4. Resume the worker agent to address feedback (push fix commits)
5. Re-verify: `bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`

## Phase 3: Merge (wave order)

For each wave:
1. Squash-merge PRs in wave using `mcp__plugin_github_github__merge_pull_request` with `merge_method: "squash"`
2. After wave merges, later-wave PR branches need conflict resolution (rebase or merge main into branch)
3. Re-run CI on updated branches before merging next wave

## File Conflict Map

| File | Issues | Resolution |
|------|--------|------------|
| `config.ts` | All 10 | Each adds distinct schema section. Textual conflicts at merge. |
| `types.ts` | #6, #12 | #6 adds metadata fields, #12 adds source type. Orthogonal. |
| `github.ts` | #6, #7, #13 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 2: #7. Sequential. |
| `llm.ts` | #6, #10, #13, #15 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 2: #15, Wave 3: #10. Sequential. |
| `issues.ts` | #6, #11, #13 | Wave 1: #6+#13, Wave 3: #11. Sequential. |
| `index.ts` | #12, #14 | Wave 4: both. Orthogonal additions. |

## Verification

Per-PR: `bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`
Per-wave (after merge to main): `bun install && bun run lint && bun test && bun run build`
Final: All 84+ original tests pass + new tests for all 10 features.

allowedPrompts.prompt: git merge, resolve conflicts, rebuild dist with npm, run tests and lint
git add, commit, and push resolved conflicts
run bun test, bun run lint, npm run build, npm ci

input.plan:
# Plan: Resolve Merge Conflicts and Continue Wave Merges

## Context

10 PRs (#16-#25) were implemented in parallel worktrees for issues #6-#15. PR #16 (Metadata, issue #6) was squash-merged to main. All 9 remaining PRs now have merge conflicts against updated main. CI is also failing because `dist/` was built with `bun` but CI uses `npm` (different ncc chunk IDs).

## Current State

- **Merged**: PR #16 (feat/6-extend-metadata) → main (adds `license`, `archived`, `fork`, `owner`, `homepage`, `lastPushedAt` to metadata)
- **Open PRs**: #17-#25 (9 PRs), all with merge conflicts
- **CI**: Failing on all PRs (dist/ freshness + merge conflicts)

## Conflict Analysis

| PR | Worktree | Real Source Conflicts | Dist Only? |
|----|----------|----------------------|------------|
| #23 | agent-ac8458d4 | `github.ts`, `llm.ts`, `issues.ts` | No |
| #17 | agent-a7f40194 | `github.ts` | No |
| #19 | agent-a4453357 | `types.ts` | No |
| #22 | agent-a3b44974 | `types.ts` | No |
| #18 | agent-adefdbe8 | None | Yes |
| #20 | agent-a0fbbae4 | None | Yes |
| #21 | agent-a98e9a58 | None | Yes |
| #24 | agent-a9ec4326 | None | Yes |
| #25 | agent-a4b467f3 | None | Yes |

## Execution Plan

### For each of the 9 PRs (process in wave order):

```bash
cd /Users/moventsai/Projects/mine/awesome-list-radar/.claude/worktrees/<agent-dir>
git fetch origin main
git merge origin/main    # will report conflicts
```

Then resolve conflicts per-PR:

**Wave 1 remaining — PR #23 (Retry):**
- `github.ts`: Main added metadata fields (`license`, `archived`, etc.) in candidate push. PR #23 wraps `client.search.repos()` with `withRetry()`. Resolution: keep both — metadata fields AND retry wrapper.
- `llm.ts`: Main added XML tags for new metadata. PR #23 wraps `anthropic.messages.create()` with `withRetry()`. Resolution: keep both.
- `issues.ts`: Main added table rows for new metadata. PR #23 wraps `listIssues`/`createIssue` with `withRetry()`. Resolution: keep both.

**Wave 2 — PR #17 (Pagination):**
- `github.ts`: Main added metadata fields in single-page fetch. PR #17 replaces with pagination loop. Resolution: pagination loop must include the new metadata fields (`license`, `archived`, `fork`, `owner`, `homepage`, `lastPushedAt`) in candidate push inside the loop.

**Wave 2 — PRs #18, #25:** dist/ only — merge main, delete dist/, rebuild with `npm`.

**Wave 3 — PR #19 (Filter):**
- `types.ts`: Main added 6 fields. PR #19 also added `fork?`, `archived?`, `license?`, `lastCommitAt?`. Resolution: take main's version (superset; use `lastPushedAt` not `lastCommitAt`). Remove duplicates PR #19 added.

**Wave 3 — PRs #20, #21:** dist/ only — merge main, rebuild.

**Wave 4 — PR #22 (Registry):**
- `types.ts`: Main added metadata fields. PR #22 added `"registry"` to source union. Resolution: keep both changes (orthogonal).

**Wave 4 — PR #24:** dist/ only — merge main, rebuild.

### After conflict resolution for each PR:

```bash
# Resolve source conflicts manually
git add <resolved-files>
# Rebuild dist/ with npm (matches CI)
rm -rf dist/
npm ci && npm run build
# Verify
bun run lint && bun test
# Commit and push
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: merge main and resolve conflicts"
git push
```

### Then continue wave merges:

1. Squash-merge Wave 1 remaining (PR #23)
2. Merge main into Wave 2 PRs, resolve, squash-merge (#17, #18, #25)
3. Merge main into Wave 3 PRs, resolve, squash-merge (#19, #20, #21)
4. Merge main into Wave 4 PRs, resolve, squash-merge (#22, #24)

## Key Files

- Worktree base: `/Users/moventsai/Projects/mine/awesome-list-radar/.claude/worktrees/`
- Main `github.ts` (post-merge): has metadata fields + single-page fetch
- Main `types.ts` (post-merge): has `lastPushedAt`, `license`, `archived`, `fork`, `owner`, `homepage`

## Verification

Per-PR after conflict resolution:
1. `bun run lint` — type check passes
2. `bun test` — all tests pass
3. `npm run build` — dist/ built with npm for CI compat
4. Push → CI should pass
